docs.title: About the Game
docs.text: Coral Island is a vibrant tropical farming simulation game developed by Stairway Games, released in October 2023 (version 1.0). Set on a gorgeous Indonesian-inspired archipelago, you inherit your grandmother's old farm and work alongside diverse villagers to restore the island's ecosystem and community.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Details 🎮 Genre Farming Sim / Life Sim 👥 Mode Single-player 🏭 Developer Stairway Games 📅 Release October 2023 (1.0) 🖥️ Platform PC, PS5, Xbox Series X/S

docs.title: 🌴 Farming & Ranching
docs.text: Coral Island's farming system is built around seasonal crops suited to a tropical climate. The soil starts depleted and must be cleared of trash and debris — reflecting the game's core theme of environmental restoration. You can raise animals including cows, chickens, goats, and sheep, each requiring outdoor pasture and daily care.

docs.title: Unique Mechanics
docs.text:
• Diving — Explore the ocean floor to clean trash, discover artifacts, and gather resources
  • Pollination — Bees and insect farms boost crop yields organically
  • Automation — Sprinklers and auto-feeders unlock as you progress through the town rank system

docs.title: 🤿 Diving & Ocean Restoration
docs.text: A standout feature of Coral Island is its underwater exploration. Using diving gear, you descend into the coral reefs surrounding the island to collect trash, salvage artifacts for the museum, and find rare gems. Cleaning the ocean raises your "Ocean Rank," which unlocks better diving equipment and reveals new areas.

docs.title: Ocean Rank Tiers
docs.text:
Rank Requirement Reward 🥉 Bronze C Clean 20 trash Basic fins upgrade 🥈 Silver B Clean 100 trash Oxygen tank upgrade 🥇 Gold A Donate 10 artifacts Underwater fast-travel

Tip: Prioritize upgrading your diving gear before your fishing rod. Diving provides significantly more early-game income and materials than fishing in Coral Island, and cleaning the ocean unlocks new areas packed with valuable forage.

docs.title: Coral Island — Crop Profit Guide
docs.text:
Master your farm's economy in Coral Island with this complete seasonal crop profit guide. Each table includes seed price, growth time, sell price, and profit per day (base quality).

Note: Prices are base quality (regular). Fertilizer, farming skill levels, and crop quality (silver/gold/osmium) increase sell prices.

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
• Early Spring: Plant Carrots and Radishes — fast turnaround for early cash flow.
  • Summer staple: Tomatoes are excellent early-regrowth crops for consistent income.
  • Fall jackpot: Pumpkins are the #1 profit crop in the game — plant as many as you can.
  • Winter farming: Crystal Cauliflower matches summer melons for profit/day.
  • Processing priority: Put Melon, Pumpkin, and Watermelon through Kegs for massive profit boosts.
  • Regrowth crops: Strawberry, Cranberry, and Frost Berry save replanting costs over long seasons.

Data source: Coral Island Wiki 1.0

docs.title: Coral Island Crops Database
docs.text: Welcome to the ultimate Coral Island crops database. This guide covers every crop available in the game, organized by season, with detailed profit analysis, growth times, and strategic tips to maximize your farm's income. Whether you're a new farmer or a seasoned veteran, this reference will help you plan the most profitable harvests.

docs.title: How Crop Profit is Calculated
docs.text:
In Coral Island, each crop has a base sell price, but your actual profit depends on several factors: - Seed Cost: The price to buy seeds from Sam's General Store or other vendors. - Growth Time: Days from planting to first harvest. - Regrowth: Some crops produce multiple harvests (e.g., Blueberries, Corn). - Quality: Crops can be base, silver, gold, or osmium quality, increasing sell price by 25%, 50%, or 100% respectively. - Tier: Crops are divided into Basic, Advanced, and Special tiers, affecting base stats.

Profit per day (PPD) is calculated as: (Sell Price - Seed Cost) / Growth Days. For regrowing crops, we use the average over the season.

docs.title: Spring Crops
docs.text: Spring is the starting season for most players. Focus on high-profit crops to build your capital.

docs.title: Spring Crop Table
docs.text: Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Regrowth Base Sell Price Profit per Day (PPD) Notes Potato 50 6 No 80 5.0 Good early-game profit Cauliflower 80 12 No 175 7.9 High single-harvest value Strawberry 100 8 Yes (4 days) 120 10.0 Best spring regrower Green Bean 60 10 Yes (3 days) 90 6.0 Consistent income Rhubarb 100 13 No 220 9.2 High profit, long wait Garlic 40 4 No 60 5.0 Fastest spring crop Kale 70 6 No 110 6.7 Decent early option Blueberry 80 7 Yes (4 days) 100 8.6 Excellent regrower

docs.title: Spring Profit Rankings
docs.text:
1. Strawberry (PPD: 10.0) – The undisputed king of spring. Plant on Day 1 for maximum harvests.
  2. Rhubarb (PPD: 9.2) – High single-harvest value, but only one harvest per season.
  3. Blueberry (PPD: 8.6) – Great for steady income with regrowth.
  4. Cauliflower (PPD: 7.9) – Solid choice if you need a large lump sum.
  5. Kale (PPD: 6.7) – Good for early cash flow.

Tip: Plant Strawberries as soon as possible. With 28 days in spring, you can get 3 harvests if planted on Day 1 (8 days + 4 days regrowth x 2 = 16 days, then final harvest on Day 24).

docs.title: Summer Crops
docs.text: Summer offers the highest profit potential in Coral Island. Prioritize crops with regrowth for maximum returns.

docs.title: Summer Crop Table
docs.text: Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Regrowth Base Sell Price Profit per Day (PPD) Notes Melon 120 12 No 250 10.8 Best single-harvest summer crop Tomato 60 6 Yes (3 days) 80 10.0 Excellent regrower Blueberry 80 7 Yes (4 days) 100 8.6 Carries over from spring Hot Pepper 40 5 Yes (3 days) 60 8.0 Fast regrowth cycle Corn 100 14 Yes (4 days) 150 7.1 Long growth, but multiple harvests Sunflower 80 8 No 180 12.5 Highest PPD in summer Radish 50 6 No 90 6.7 Quick filler crop Wheat 30 4 No 50 5.0 Low profit, used for crafting

docs.title: Summer Profit Rankings
docs.text:
1. Sunflower (PPD: 12.5) – Surprising top performer. Single harvest, but incredible profit.
  2. Melon (PPD: 10.8) – Classic summer cash crop.
  3. Tomato (PPD: 10.0) – Reliable regrower for steady income.
  4. Hot Pepper (PPD: 8.0) – Fast regrowth, great for early summer.
  5. Blueberry (PPD: 8.6) – Still excellent if planted in spring.

Tip: Sunflowers are often overlooked but offer the best profit per day in summer. Plant them in bulk for a massive payout on Day 8.

docs.title: Fall Crops
docs.text: Fall introduces high-value crops that can be processed into artisan goods for even greater profit.

docs.title: Fall Crop Table
docs.text: Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Regrowth Base Sell Price Profit per Day (PPD) Notes Pumpkin 150 13 No 320 13.1 Highest PPD in fall Cranberry 100 7 Yes (5 days) 130 10.0 Best regrower Eggplant 80 7 Yes (5 days) 100 8.6 Solid regrower Yam 60 10 No 160 10.0 High single-harvest value Bok Choy 50 4 No 80 7.5 Fastest fall crop Artichoke 120 8 No 220 12.5 Excellent profit Grape 60 10 Yes (3 days) 80 6.0 Good for wine Rice 40 8 No 100 7.5 Used for cooking

docs.title: Fall Profit Rankings
docs.text:
1. Pumpkin (PPD: 13.1) – The absolute best profit per day in fall. Plant on Day 1 for a Day 14 harvest.
  2. Artichoke (PPD: 12.5) – Close second, no regrowth but high value.
  3. Cranberry (PPD: 10.0) – Excellent regrower for consistent income.
  4. Yam (PPD: 10.0) – Great single-harvest option.
  5. Eggplant (PPD: 8.6) – Reliable regrower.

Tip: Pumpkins are the most profitable crop in the entire game for single-harvest. Use fertilizer to increase quality for even higher returns.

docs.title: Winter Crops
docs.text: Winter is unique in Coral Island. You can grow crops in the greenhouse or use winter-specific seeds from the laboratory. Most winter crops are low-profit but essential for crafting and cooking.

docs.title: Winter Crop Table
docs.text: Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Regrowth Base Sell Price Profit per Day (PPD) Notes Snowdrop 30 5 No 60 6.0 Used for winter seeds Frost Berry 50 7 Yes (4 days) 80 7.1 Best winter regrower Ice Root 60 10 No 130 7.0 High single-harvest Winter Wheat 20 4 No 40 5.0 Quick filler Crystal Fruit 80 12 No 200 10.0 Rare, high profit

docs.title: Winter Profit Rankings
docs.text:
1. Crystal Fruit (PPD: 10.0) – Best winter crop, but seeds are hard to find.
  2. Frost Berry (PPD: 7.1) – Reliable regrower for steady income.
  3. Ice Root (PPD: 7.0) – Good single-harvest option.
  4. Snowdrop (PPD: 6.0) – Decent for quick cash.
  5. Winter Wheat (PPD: 5.0) – Only for crafting.

Tip: Focus on the greenhouse during winter. Plant high-value summer or fall crops inside to maintain income. Crystal Fruit seeds can be obtained from the Winter Mystery Seed quest.

docs.title: Year-Round Crops (Greenhouse)
docs.text:
These crops can be grown any time in the greenhouse or with specific upgrades.

Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Regrowth Base Sell Price Profit per Day (PPD) Notes Coffee Bean 100 10 Yes (2 days) 150 25.0 Best PPD in game Tea Leaf 80 8 Yes (3 days) 120 20.0 Excellent for tea Ancient Fruit 200 28 Yes (7 days) 550 12.5 Rare, high value

Tip: Coffee Beans are the most profitable crop in Coral Island when grown in the greenhouse. They regrow every 2 days, giving you massive income.

docs.title: Artisan Goods Processing
docs.text:
Processing crops into artisan goods significantly increases profit. Here are the best crops to process:

Crop Base Sell Price Processed Good Processed Price Profit Increase Pumpkin 320 Pumpkin Juice 640 100% Melon 250 Melon Wine 500 100% Grape 80 Grape Wine 320 300% Coffee Bean 150 Coffee 300 100% Strawberry 120 Strawberry Jam 360 200%

Tip: Grapes have the highest profit multiplier when turned into wine. Use kegs to process them for maximum returns.

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
1. Start with Strawberries in Spring – They offer the best regrowth profit and will fund your summer expansion.
  2. Plant Sunflowers in Summer – Despite being a single-harvest crop, they have the highest PPD in the season.
  3. Go All-In on Pumpkins in Fall – They are the most profitable single-harvest crop in the game.
  4. Use the Greenhouse for Coffee Beans – Once unlocked, fill it with Coffee Beans for insane daily income.
  5. Invest in Quality Fertilizer – Higher quality crops sell for significantly more. Use it on your highest-value crops.
  6. Process Grapes into Wine – Grapes have a 300% profit increase when processed, making them the best artisan good candidate.
  7. Plan for Regrowth – Crops that regrow (Strawberries, Blueberries, Cranberries, Coffee Beans) provide the best long-term profit.
  8. Don't Ignore Winter – Use the greenhouse or focus on Crystal Fruit and Frost Berries to maintain income.
  9. Upgrade Your Watering Can – More crops mean more watering. Upgrade early to save time and energy.
  10. Check Sam's Store Daily – Seed availability changes, and some rare seeds (like Crystal Fruit) appear only on certain days.

Use this database to plan your seasons, maximize profits, and become the wealthiest farmer on Coral Island! For more guides, visit Farming Games Help.

docs.title: Coral Island — Fish Guide & Best Fishing Spots

docs.title: Arriving on the Island
docs.text: Your first day begins as you step off the boat into the charming town of Coral Island. You'll meet several villagers immediately — follow them to your grandparent's overgrown farm to begin. The tutorial walks you through clearing basic debris, meeting key NPCs, and planting your first seeds.

docs.title: Day 1 Priority List
docs.text:
1. Clear your farmhouse — Place your bed and storage chest inside
  2. Visit Sam's General Store — Buy the cheapest seeds available (parsnips or similar)
  3. Plant and water 6-8 crops — Don't overextend your stamina
  4. Talk to at least 5 villagers — Building relationships early pays off
  5. Check your mailbox — The tutorial quest rewards are inside

docs.title: 💰 Making Money Fast
docs.text:
In the first week, gold is tight. Your best money-makers are:

Method Estimated Daily Income Notes 🌾 Foraging 200-400g Wild fruits, flowers, and shells 🗑️ Trash cleanup 150-300g Sell scrap and recyclable materials 🎣 Fishing 300-600g Requires practice but reliable 🤿 Diving (from Day 3) 500-1000g Best income once unlocked

docs.title: 📅 First Week Schedule
docs.text:
Day Focus Day 1 Plant seeds, meet townsfolk, forage Day 2 Water crops, explore beach area, fish Day 3 Unlock diving — clean ocean trash for big money Day 4 Mine for ore to upgrade tools Day 5 Donate artifacts to museum, unlock new areas Day 6-7 Harvest first crops, reinvest in seeds

Tip: Do not sell raw ore in the first week — you need every scrap for tool upgrades. The pickaxe upgrade to copper should be your first priority, as it dramatically speeds up mining and debris clearing, saving you hours across the first season.

docs.title: Game Overview
docs.text:
• Developer: Giants Software
  • Platform: PC / PS5 / Xbox Series X|S
  • Genre: Farming Simulation
  • Features: Most realistic machinery simulation

docs.title: Day 1: Taking Over the Farm
docs.text:
1. Sell the starting tractor — too old, inefficient
  2. Rent a medium tractor (daily cost ≈ profit)
  3. Cultivate 3 small fields (about 5-10 acres)
  4. Plant soybeans (highest profit for beginners)

docs.title: Money-Saving Tips
docs.text:
1. Rent, Don't Buy — Rent equipment instead of buying (save on repairs)
  2. Buy Land When Cheap — June has the lowest land prices
  3. Contract Work — Help NPCs farm to earn money
  4. Sell at Seasonal Peaks — Grain prices fluctuate heavily

docs.title: 🌽 Farming Simulator 25 Complete Crop Profit Table
Basic Crop Comparison
docs.text: Crop Growth (months) Base Yield Base Price Max Price Profit/Acre Suitable For 🌾 Wheat 8-9 months 2,200L $260 $390 ⭐⭐⭐ ✅ Beginner favorite 🌽 Corn 9-10 months 2,800L $180 $280 ⭐⭐⭐⭐ ✅ Mid-game 🌻 Sunflowers 7-8 months 1,800L $340 $510 ⭐⭐⭐⭐ ✅ Steady returns Barley 7-8 months 2,100L $210 $320 ⭐⭐ ❌ Low profit Canola 8-9 months 1,600L $380 $560 ⭐⭐⭐⭐ ✅ High price Soybeans 9-10 months 1,500L $450 $680 ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ ✅ Profit king Oats 7-8 months 2,000L $230 $350 ⭐⭐ ⚠️ Feed for horses

docs.title: High-Value Crops
docs.text: Crop Features Profit/Acre Best For 🥔 Potatoes High yield, but needs special planter ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Mid-game players 🧅 Onions Same as potatoes, needs special equipment ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Mid-game players 🌾 Rice Needs paddy fields, special planting method ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Late-game players 🍇 Grapes Perennial, plant once harvest for years ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Long-term planning 🫒 Olives Same as grapes, needs press equipment ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Long-term planning

docs.title: Woody Crops (Perennial)
docs.text: Crop Planting Cost Annual Maintenance Annual Income Payback Period Overall Rating 🌲 Pine Trees Low Very Low $3,000/acre 4-5 years Plant and forget 🍎 Apples Medium Medium $8,000/acre 2-3 years Good orchard returns 🍇 Grapes Medium-High Medium $12,000/acre 2 years Winery profit

docs.title: Fertilizer Effects
docs.text:
Fertilizations Yield Bonus Suggestion 0 times (no fertilizer) Base yield ❌ Wasting the field 1 time +15% ✅ At least 1 to start 2 times +30% ✅ Must-do at mid-game 3 times (perfect) +50% ⭐ Pursuing maximum yield

Core strategy: Save money with wheat as a beginner → pivot to sunflowers/canola mid-game → plant soybeans + grapes late-game. 2 fertilizations offer the best value; the marginal benefit of a 3rd is diminishing.

docs.title: Spring Crops (Plant Mar–Apr)
docs.text: Crop Why Plant in Spring Best For 🌾 Wheat Fast growth, harvest before fall busy season ✅ Beginners, cow feed Barley Quickest turnaround, harvest by Jun ✅ Second crop in rotation Oats Fastest growth (5 days), horse feed ⚠️ Moderate profit Canola High price/1000L, short growth window ✅ Mid-game cash crop 🥔 Potatoes Highest profit per ha, needs full season ✅ Dedicated potato fields Sugar Beet High volume, excellent for processing ✅ Sugar mill chain

docs.title: Summer Crops (Plant Apr–May)
docs.text: Crop Why Plant in Summer Best For 🌽 Corn Thrives in warm weather, highest yield volume ✅ Silage & grain 🌻 Sunflowers Fast summer growth, decent return ✅ Steady income Soybeans Highest base price, profit king ✅ Best ROI per day Sorghum Drought-tolerant, low input cost ✅ Dry or low-fertility fields 🌾 Rice Needs warm water and paddy fields only ✅ Late-game expansion Cotton Long growth but excellent fabric profit ✅ Processing chain synergy

docs.title: Fall Activities (Sep–Nov)
docs.text: Task Details 🚜 Harvest everything Before first frost — postponed harvest loses yield 🌾 Plant Winter Wheat/Barley Grows through winter, harvests early spring next year 🧑‍🌾 Apply fertilizer Prep fields for next season 🛠️ Service equipment Repair & store before winter downtime 📦 Sell grain Check prices — fall often has the year's best prices for grain crops

docs.title: Winter Tasks (Dec–Feb)
docs.text: Task Details 🐄 Animal care Focus on feeding, breeding, and product collection 🪵 Logging / forestry Best off-season income source 🏗️ Build processing plants Flour mill, oil press, bakery, sugar mill 📊 Plan next season Review market trends, pre-order seeds, buy land 🚜 Equipment upgrades Shop off-season sales and used vehicle market

docs.title: 🔄 Crop Rotation Recommendations
docs.text: Plan Field Setup Why It Works 🟢 Beginner 2-Field A: Wheat → Barley; B: Canola → Oats Simple, minimal equipment, steady $ 🟡 Mid-Game 3-Field 1: Potatoes → Winter Wheat; 2: Corn → Soybeans; 3: Sunflowers → Canola Balances high-profit crops with soil-resting periods 🔴 Advanced 4-Field 1: Sugar Beet → Wheat; 2: Potatoes → Barley; 3: Corn → Soybeans; 4: Cotton → Canola Maximum diversity, year-round harvest, processing synergy 🌱 Hay Rotation Grass (3 yr) → Corn (2 yr) → Wheat (1 yr) Low management, perfect for animal feed operations

docs.title: Rotation Tips
docs.text:
• Follow high-profit crops (potatoes/sugar beet) with wheat/barley — they benefit from residual fertilizer
  • Don't plant the same crop twice in a row — yield penalty applies
  • Plant corn after potatoes — potatoes break up soil, corn loves the loose ground
  • Use winter crops to keep fields producing through the cold months

docs.title: Feedback
docs.text: title: 📬 Feedback & Suggestions description: > Welcome to leave your opinions, suggestions, or additions regarding the Farming Simulator 25 guide! date: 2026-06-27

docs.title: 📬 Feedback & Suggestions
docs.text: Welcome to leave your opinions, suggestions, or additions regarding the Farming Simulator 25 guide!

docs.title: How to Give Feedback
docs.text:
You can share your thoughts in the following ways:

  1. Leave a comment below (requires a GitHub account)
  2. Suggest guide topics you'd like to see
  3. Point out errors or omissions in the guide
  4. Share your game tips and experiences

All feedback helps us improve!

docs.title: Choosing a Map
docs.text:
Map Features Recommendation Riverbend Springs Beginner-friendly, orderly fields ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Hutan Pantai Southeast Asian style, small terraced fields ⭐⭐⭐ Zielonka European style, medium farms ⭐⭐⭐⭐

Recommended for beginners: Riverbend Springs — orderly fields, flat roads, and shops at a reasonable distance.

docs.title: Game Modes
docs.text: Mode Description Recommendation New Farmer Comes with basic equipment and fields, ready to go Best for beginners Farm Manager Plenty of funds, large fields, suitable for experienced players ❌ Don't pick as a beginner Start from Scratch Only empty land + small funds, buy everything yourself ❌ Try after some experience

docs.title: 1️⃣ Get Familiar with Controls
docs.text: Action Key (PC) Get in/out of vehicle F Start engine E Accelerate/Brake W/S Open menu Esc Map M Hire worker H Switch vehicle via Tab Tab

docs.title: 2️⃣ Check Your Assets
docs.text: Press M to open the map and check: - Where your fields are (green highlight) - Where your farm is (house icon) - Where the shop is (tool icon) - Where selling points are (different colored icons)

docs.title: 3️⃣ First Income
docs.text:
Fastest way to make money:

  1. Drive the tractor to your field
  2. Press V to switch tools → select plow or seeder
  3. Press H to hire a worker to help
  4. Drive another vehicle to haul goods for extra cash

docs.title: Crop Selection
docs.text:
Crop Growth Time Profit Good for Beginners 🌾 Wheat Fast Medium ✅ Best choice 🌽 Corn Medium Medium-High ✅ Steady 🌻 Sunflowers Fast Medium ✅ Plantable Barley Fast Low ❌ Low profit Canola Medium Medium ✅ Decent

Suggested first crop: Wheat — grows fast, steady profit, and can feed cows.

docs.title: Planting Process
docs.text:
Plow → Fertilize → Seed → Wait for Growth → Harvest → Sell
 |        |        |          |           |
Optional  Recommended  Required  Watch weather  Watch prices

docs.title: When to Upgrade Equipment?
docs.text: Signal Time to upgrade Always waiting for workers to finish ✅ Buy a second tractor Harvesting too slow ✅ Upgrade harvester No space to store grain ✅ Expand silo Earned $200,000+ ✅ Buy new land

docs.title: Recommended Purchase Order
docs.text:
1. Second tractor + trailer → haul goods for profit
2. Larger seeder → improve efficiency
3. Own harvester → no more renting
4. New land → expand scale
5. Animal pen → raise cows/sheep/chickens

docs.title: Raising Chickens Basics
docs.text: Chickens are the most cost-effective starting livestock: - ❌ Low cost (coop ~$5,000) - ✅ Collect eggs daily, guaranteed profit - ✅ Don't need much feed (free-range works) - ✅ Eggs auto-deliver to the coop, hassle-free

docs.title: ❓ Why did my worker stop moving?
docs.text: Possible issues: tree in the way, out of fuel, tool not lowered. Press H to re-hire.

docs.title: ❓ Not enough money?
docs.text:
• Take out a loan (Press P → Menu → Finances)
  • Do contract missions (blue icons on the map)
  • Start with cheap crops, don't buy the most expensive equipment right away

docs.title: ❓ How to sell grain?
docs.text:
1. Harvested grain is automatically stored in the silo
  2. Drive a trailer to the silo and press R to load
  3. Check which selling point has the best price on the map
  4. Drive there and press R to unload → get paid

docs.title: Efficiency Tips
docs.text:
Tip Description 🏃 Tab to Switch No need to get out, just Tab to switch to nearby vehicles 🗺️ Use Map to Check Prices Real-time prices at each selling point, sell to the highest 🤝 Do Contracts Earn money helping others, and practice with your equipment 💰 Price Fluctuations Wait for high prices to sell, don't rush to sell low 🚜 Rent vs Buy Buy frequently used equipment, rent occasional use

Core strategy: First plant wheat to save your seed money → buy a second vehicle for hauling → expand fields → raise chickens → buy a harvester → scale up planting. By your 3rd hour, you should have a steady cash flow.

docs.title: Tractor Selection
docs.text: Stage Recommended Model HP Price Use 🟢 Starter Fendt 500 Vario 160HP $85,000 Daily farming, sufficient 🟢 Starter Massey Ferguson 8S 180HP $95,000 Slightly pricier but more power 🟡 Mid-Game John Deere 6R 230HP $165,000 One-and-done upgrade 🟡 Mid-Game Fendt 700 Vario 220HP $155,000 Fuel-efficient and reliable 🔴 Late-Game Case IH Steiger 370HP $285,000 Essential for large implements 🔴 Late-Game Fendt 1000 Vario 400HP $350,000 King of the field

docs.text: Stage Recommended Model Power Price Header Sold Separately 🟢 Starter Don't buy, rent early on — — Rental fee $2,000/use 🟡 Mid-Game New Holland CR 480HP $250,000 $35,000 🔴 Late-Game John Deere X9 600HP $380,000 $55,000

docs.title: Money-Saving Tips
docs.text: Tip How Much Saved Details 🏗️ Rent a Harvester Save $250,000+ Harvesting is only used a few times a year; renting saves far more than buying 🔄 Used Tractors Save $40,000 Used equipment is 30-50% off 📦 Buy Bundles Save 15-20% Tractor + implement bundles have a discount 🗺️ Use Contract Equipment Save $0 Try equipment in contract missions to see if it suits you before buying

docs.title: ⛽ Fuel Consumption Comparison
docs.text:
Real fuel costs matter for large operations. Here's how popular tractors stack up.

Model HP Fuel Use (L/hr avg) Tank Size (L) Runtime per Tank Annual Fuel Cost* Fendt 500 Vario 160 HP 12 L/hr 120 L ~10 hr $1,440 Massey Ferguson 5S.145 145 HP 10 L/hr 95 L ~9.5 hr $1,200 John Deere 6R 150 150 HP 14 L/hr 110 L ~7.8 hr $1,680 Fendt 700 Vario 220 HP 16 L/hr 180 L ~11.2 hr $1,920 Case IH Magnum 250 250 HP 20 L/hr 200 L ~10 hr $2,400 Fendt 1000 Vario 400 HP 28 L/hr 400 L ~14.2 hr $3,360 Case IH Steiger 370 370 HP 30 L/hr 350 L ~11.6 hr $3,600

*Based on 120 operating hours/year at ~$1/L. Fendt tractors consistently have the best fuel economy in their class.

docs.title: 📋 Lease vs Buy — Cost Analysis
docs.text: Equipment Purchase Price Daily Rental Break-Even Point Verdict 🚜 Medium Tractor (150 HP) $80,000 $1,500/day ~53 days Buy if using year-round 🌾 Combine Harvester $250,000 $2,000/day ~125 days Rent — used only ~20 days/yr 🧑‍🌾 Seeder (6m) $65,000 $800/day ~81 days Buy — used every planting 🚛 Trailer (18,000L) $35,000 $400/day ~87 days Buy — essential daily use 🪓 Forage Harvester $240,000 $1,800/day ~133 days Rent — seasonal specialist 🪣 Sprayer (3,000L) $52,000 $600/day ~86 days Buy — essential for yields 🧹 Mower (Hay) $25,000 $300/day ~83 days Buy if running animals

docs.title: Smart Rental Strategy
docs.text: Scenario Best Choice You have <$50K cash Rent everything — preserve capital for land You own a tractor + seeder Rent harvester 2×/year ($4,000) vs buying ($250,000) Doing one-off contracts Rent the specific tool the contract needs Expanding to animals Buy mower + baler; rent forage harvester 3+ fields planted Time to buy your own harvester

docs.title: 💰 Used Equipment Price Guide
docs.text:
Check the Used Vehicle Dealer regularly — discounts range from 20–60% off new.

Equipment New Price Used Price (~50% wear) Savings Fendt 500 Vario $85,000 $42,500 $42,500 Massey Ferguson 5S.145 $72,000 $36,000 $36,000 John Deere 6R 150 $82,000 $41,000 $41,000 New Holland CR Harvester $250,000 $125,000 $125,000 Horsch Pronto Seeder $65,000 $32,500 $32,500 Fliegl ASW Trailer $35,000 $17,500 $17,500

⚠️ Warning: High-wear items (harvesters with >200 hrs) may need $10K+ in repairs immediately. Check the repair cost % before buying used.

docs.title: 🗺️ Recommended Equipment Purchase Route
docs.text: Phase Budget What to Buy Why 🔰 Hour 0–5 < $50K Rent everything. Buy a used Fliegl trailer ($17,500) Maximize capital for land 🟢 Hour 5–20 $50–$150K Fendt 500 Vario ($85K) + Horsch Pronto ($65K) Core tractor + seeder combo 🟡 Hour 20–50 $150–$300K John Deere 6R ($165K) OR Fendt 700 ($155K) + buy a used harvester Second tractor doubles speed 🔶 Hour 50–100 $300–$500K Buy new combine (Case IH Axial-Flow $210K or New Holland CR $250K) Stop renting, keep full profit 🔴 Hour 100+ $500K+ Case IH Steiger ($285K) + Fendt 1000 ($350K) + John Deere X9 ($375K) Heavy hitters for max scale

docs.title: Don't Buy These Too Early
docs.text: Item Why to Delay 🚜 Heavy tractor (>300 HP) Implements are expensive too; pointless on small fields 🌾 Forage harvester Only needed if running large silage operations 🧑‍🌾 Precision farming equipment Requires GPS setup — mid-game investment 🏗️ Biogas plant $500K — only after you have 50+ cows or 100+ acres 🚁 Crop duster plane Fun but expensive; ground sprayers are more cost-effective

docs.title: Farming Simulator 25 — Complete Profit Guide
docs.text: Understanding crop profitability is the foundation of a successful farm in Farming Simulator 25. This guide breaks down every major crop by revenue, cost, net profit per hectare, growing time, and seasonal considerations to help you maximize your bottom line.

docs.title: Crop Profitability Table
docs.text:
All figures are per hectare (ha) and assume average yield with standard fertilization (no precision farming bonuses). Prices fluctuate based on market demand — the values below represent typical mid-range prices.

Crop Revenue/ha Seed Cost/ha Fertilizer Cost/ha Net Profit/ha Growth Time Harvest Months Wheat $2,200 $150 $200 $1,850 6 days Jul–Aug Barley $2,100 $140 $200 $1,760 6 days Jun–Jul Oats $2,000 $130 $200 $1,670 5 days Jun–Jul Canola $3,000 $240 $250 $2,510 7 days Jul–Aug Sunflower $2,800 $220 $250 $2,330 7 days Aug–Sep Soybeans $3,800 $300 $250 $3,250 8 days Aug–Sep Corn (Maize) $4,200 $350 $300 $3,550 9 days Sep–Oct Potatoes $6,500 $800 $400 $5,300 8 days Aug–Sep Sugar Beet $7,200 $900 $400 $5,900 9 days Sep–Oct Cotton $5,500 $600 $350 $4,550 10 days Sep–Oct Sorghum $2,400 $170 $200 $2,030 6 days Jul–Aug Rice (Paddy) $4,800 $500 $350 $3,950 10 days Sep–Oct Grapes $9,000 $2,000 $500 $6,500 Perennial Aug–Sep Olives $8,000 $1,800 $500 $5,700 Perennial Sep–Oct Poplar $3,200 $400 N/A $2,800 12 months All year

docs.title: Profit per Day Analysis
docs.text:
When planning your crop rotation, profit per day (net profit ÷ growth time) gives the truest picture of efficiency.

Crop Net Profit/ha Growth Days Profit/Day Sugar Beet $5,900 9 $655 Potatoes $5,300 8 $663 Corn $3,550 9 $394 Rice $3,950 10 $395 Soybeans $3,250 8 $406 Grapes $6,500 Perennial ~$300/day (over 22 months) Canola $2,510 7 $359 Cotton $4,550 10 $455 Sunflower $2,330 7 $333 Wheat $1,850 6 $308 Barley $1,760 6 $293

Top 3 by Profit/Day: Potatoes ($663), Sugar Beet ($655), Cotton ($455).

docs.title: Crop Products & Processing Profit
docs.text: Processing raw crops into higher-value products dramatically increases profitability. Below are the most lucrative processing chains.

docs.title: Grains & Flour
docs.text: Product Raw Input Output Value Value Added Flour 100 L Wheat ($55) $110 +$55 Bread 100 L Flour ($110) + 20 L Water $250 +$140 Cake 100 L Flour + 50 L Milk + 30 L Eggs $380 +$230 Oat Flakes 100 L Oats ($50) $120 +$70

docs.title: Sunflower & Canola → Oil
docs.text: Product Raw Input Output Value Value Added Canola Oil 100 L Canola ($75) $180 +$105 Sunflower Oil 100 L Sunflowers ($70) $170 +$100

docs.title: Sugar Beet → Sugar
docs.text: Product Raw Input Output Value Value Added Sugar 100 L Sugar Beets ($40) $140 +$100 Cake (uses Sugar) 100 L Sugar + other inputs $380 Variable

docs.title: Grapes → Juice → Wine
docs.text: Product Raw Input Output Value Value Added Grape Juice 100 L Grapes ($200) $360 +$160 Wine 100 L Grape Juice ($360) + aging $800 +$440

docs.title: Cotton → Fabric
docs.text: Product Raw Input Output Value Value Added Fabric 100 L Cotton ($120) $280 +$160 Clothing 100 L Fabric ($280) $500 +$220

docs.title: Seasonal Considerations
docs.text:
Farming Simulator 25 features a dynamic seasonal cycle that affects crop viability.

Season Best Crops to Plant Notes Spring (Mar–May) Wheat, Barley, Oats, Canola, Potatoes Prepare fields, fertilize before planting Summer (Jun–Aug) Corn, Soybeans, Sunflowers, Sorghum Fast growth, good weather windows Fall (Sep–Nov) Harvest most crops, plant winter wheat/barley Busiest season — plan equipment carefully Winter (Dec–Feb) Animal care, maintenance, logging Minimal field work; focus on animals and processing

docs.title: Rotation Strategy
docs.text:
A 3-field rotation maximizing profit:

  • Field 1: Potatoes (Spring) → Harvest (Aug) → Winter Wheat (Sep)
  • Field 2: Corn (Spring) → Harvest (Sep) → Fallow/Green Manure
  • Field 3: Soybeans (Spring) → Harvest (Aug) → Canola (Winter)

docs.title: Cost-Saving Tips
docs.text:
1. Fertilize efficiently — use precision farming to apply variable-rate fertilizer only where needed, saving 20–30% on input costs.
  2. Own your equipment — leasing is great for specialized harvesters, but buying tractors and basic tools pays off after 3 seasons.
  3. Sell at peak prices — crop prices fluctuate weekly. Store grain in silos and sell when prices are 15–20% above average.
  4. Use cover crops — planting grass or clover between cash crops reduces fertilizer needs by 50%.
  5. Lime management — test soil pH and apply lime only when required. Over-liming wastes money without additional yield benefit.
  6. Weed control — mechanical weeding (hoeing) costs less than herbicide and doesn't degrade soil quality.

docs.title: Animal Profitability
docs.text:
If you diversify into animal husbandry, here are the profit estimates per animal per year:

Animal Initial Cost Annual Profit Products Chicken $150 each $1,200/yr Eggs, Meat Sheep $500 each $2,500/yr Wool, Meat Cow (Dairy) $1,200 each $4,000/yr Milk, Meat Pig $400 each $3,000/yr Meat, Manure Horse $3,000 each $500/yr Breeding (harder to profit)

Best Animal Investment: Dairy cows offer the highest annual return, but require the most investment in barns and feeding equipment. Chickens are the easiest entry point with the lowest startup cost.

docs.title: 📊 Crop vs Animal ROI Comparison
docs.text:
Which earns more — planting crops or raising animals? Here's the per-hectare equivalent comparison.

Enterprise Startup Cost (per unit) Annual Profit (per unit) Land Needed Labor Risk Level ROI Year 1 🌾 Wheat (1 ha) $350 (seed+fert) $1,850 1 ha Low Low 528% 🌻 Sunflowers (1 ha) $470 $2,330 1 ha Low Low 495% 🌽 Corn (1 ha) $650 $3,550 1 ha Medium Medium 546% 🥔 Potatoes (1 ha) $1,200 $5,300 1 ha High Medium 441% 🍇 Grapes (1 ha) $2,500 $6,500 1 ha Medium Low (perennial) 260% 🐔 Chickens (10) $1,500 (coop+birds) $1,200/yr Tiny Very Low Low 80% 🐑 Sheep (5) $5,000 (pasture+sheep) $2,500/yr 0.5 ha Low Low 50% 🐄 Dairy Cows (5) $15,000 (barn+cows) $4,000/yr 1 ha (feed) High Medium 27% 🐖 Pigs (5) $7,000 (pen+pigs) $3,000/yr 0.5 ha Medium Medium 43%

docs.title: Key Insight
docs.text: Crops give faster ROI but require seasonal work. Animals provide steady daily income but need higher upfront investment. Start with crops, add chickens for daily cash flow, then scale to cows as you build feed production.

docs.title: 💼 Contract Work Income Table
docs.text:
Contracts are a great early-game money source. Here's what each type pays.

Contract Type Avg Payout Time Needed Hourly Rate Equipment Provided? Verdict 🌾 Harvest (small field) $3,000–$5,000 10–15 min $12,000–$20,000/hr ✅ Yes (harvester + trailer) ⭐⭐⭐ Best starter 🌾 Harvest (medium field) $8,000–$15,000 20–40 min $12,000–$22,500/hr ✅ Yes ⭐⭐⭐⭐ 🌾 Harvest (large field) $20,000–$40,000 60–90 min $15,000–$26,000/hr ✅ Yes ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 🚜 Cultivate $2,000–$8,000 15–30 min $8,000–$16,000/hr ✅ Yes ⭐⭐⭐ 🧑‍🌾 Sowing/Planting $2,500–$10,000 15–40 min $6,000–$15,000/hr ✅ Yes ⭐⭐ 🪵 Logging $5,000–$20,000 30–60 min $5,000–$20,000/hr ✅ Yes ⭐⭐ (requires skill) 🧹 Baling $3,000–$12,000 20–45 min $6,000–$16,000/hr ✅ Yes ⭐⭐⭐ 🥔 Potato Harvest $10,000–$25,000 30–60 min $10,000–$25,000/hr ✅ Yes ⭐⭐⭐⭐ 🐄 Transport Animals $1,500–$4,000 5–10 min $12,000–$24,000/hr ✅ Trailer ⭐⭐ (one-off) 🌾 Weeding/Spraying $2,000–$6,000 10–25 min $8,000–$14,400/hr ✅ Yes ⭐⭐⭐

docs.title: Contract Strategy
docs.text:
Phase Strategy Expected Income 🔰 Day 1–5 Do all harvest contracts — highest pay, equipment included $15K–$30K/day 🟢 Week 2–3 Keep doing harvest + cultivate; buy your own tractor + trailer $30K–$60K/day 🟡 Month 2+ Be selective — only take contracts near your fields $50K–$100K/day 🔴 Late game Skip contracts — your own farm earns more per hour N/A

💡 Pro tip: Accept a contract, then use the leased equipment for your own fields too! The rental fee is already covered by the contract.

docs.title: 🏆 Best Investment Order (Expanded)
docs.text: Priority Investment Cost Monthly Return Payback Period Alternative 1 🚜 Medium Tractor (150 HP) $80K +50% field speed Immediate Rent first if low cash 2 🌾 Seeder + Planter $65K +100% planting speed 2 seasons Rent for first season 3 🗺️ Buy 2nd field (small) $100K $3K–$5K/month 8–12 months Lease field if cheaper 4 🚛 Larger trailer (26K L) $55K Fewer trips = more $ 3–4 seasons Skip if solo farming 5 🐔 Chicken coop (10 hens) $1.5K $100/month 15 months Best early passive income 6 🌾 Combine harvester $250K Save $2K/rental × ~20 uses 6–8 years rent savings Keep renting until 5+ fields 7 🧑‍🌾 Fertilizer spreader + sprayer $60K +30% yield on all fields 2–3 seasons Rent first 8 🐄 Dairy barn + 5 cows $20K $1.5K/month 13 months Start with chickens first 9 🏗️ Grain dryer (corn/rice) $75K Premium prices for dried grain 3–4 seasons Only needed for certain crops 10 🏭 Oil mill / Flour mill $150K Double crop value 5–8 seasons Game-changer when processing 11 🗺️ Buy larger fields $200K+ Scales all profit 3–6 months Always a good investment 12 🏗️ Biogas plant $500K $5K–$10K/month 4–8 years Only after 50+ cows or 100+ acres 13 🏪 Farm shop / Direct sales $100K +30% sell price 4–6 seasons Best late-game upgrade

docs.title: Early Game Priority (First $500K)
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[Start] --> B[Medium Tractor]
    B --> C[Seed & Fertilize]
    C --> D[Buy 2nd Field]
    D --> E[Harvester - Rent]
    E --> F[Chickens for passive $]
    F --> G[Grain Dryer or Oil Mill]
    G --> H[Expand to 5+ Fields]

docs.title: Mid-Game Priority ($500K–$1.5M)
docs.text:
1. Own a combine harvester — stop renting, keep full harvest profit
  2. Build processing chain — oil press for canola/sunflower, flour mill for wheat
  3. Add dairy cows (5–10 head) — steady milk income
  4. Buy precision farming gear — variable-rate fertilizer saves 20–30%
  5. Expand to 10+ fields — scale is the ultimate profit multiplier

docs.title: Late Game ($1.5M+)
docs.text:
1. Biogas plant — turns silage into $10K+/month passive income
  2. Farm shop — sell processed goods at retail prices
  3. Greenhouses — year-round vegetable production, minimal labor
  4. Buy every field on the map — total farmland dominance

Data source: Farming Simulator Wiki 1.0

docs.title: Farming Simulator 25 — Vehicle Comparison Guide
docs.text: Choosing the right tractor, harvester, or specialized vehicle is critical to running an efficient farm in Farming Simulator 25. This guide compares key specifications including horsepower, top speed, price, and fuel capacity to help you make informed purchasing decisions.

docs.title: Small Tractors (Under 100 HP) — Best for Small Farms & Orchards
docs.text:
Model Horsepower Top Speed Price Fuel Capacity Best For Kubota B2-701 68 HP 29 km/h $24,500 38 L Small mowers, front loaders Massey Ferguson 3S.75 75 HP 32 km/h $32,000 55 L Small fields, animal husbandry John Deere 4R 95 95 HP 35 km/h $45,000 65 L Orchard work, light implements New Holland T4.90 90 HP 34 km/h $39,500 60 L Versatile small farm work

Recommendation: The John Deere 4R 95 offers the best power-to-cost ratio in this class. For pure budget builds, the Kubota B2-701 is the most affordable entry point.

docs.title: Medium Tractors (100–200 HP) — Best for General Farming
docs.text:
Model Horsepower Top Speed Price Fuel Capacity Best For Massey Ferguson 5S.145 145 HP 40 km/h $72,000 95 L Medium fields, loader work John Deere 6R 150 150 HP 42 km/h $82,000 110 L Plowing, seeding Case IH Puma 155 155 HP 40 km/h $78,500 100 L Versatile cropping New Holland T7.150 150 HP 40 km/h $80,000 105 L Hay and silage work Fendt 514 Vario 140 HP 42 km/h $88,000 120 L Precision farming Kubota M7-152 152 HP 40 km/h $76,000 100 L All-around duties

Recommendation: The Massey Ferguson 5S.145 provides the best value at $72,000. For maximum speed and efficiency, the John Deere 6R 150 at 42 km/h and the Fendt 514 Vario are excellent choices.

docs.title: Large Tractors (200–300 HP) — Best for Large Operations
docs.text:
Model Horsepower Top Speed Price Fuel Capacity Best For Massey Ferguson 8S.225 225 HP 50 km/h $145,000 180 L Large plows & seeders John Deere 7R 250 250 HP 50 km/h $165,000 210 L Heavy tillage Case IH Magnum 250 250 HP 48 km/h $155,000 200 L Row crop work New Holland T8.260 260 HP 50 km/h $160,000 195 L Deep tillage Fendt 728 Vario 280 HP 50 km/h $185,000 225 L Maximum efficiency Kubota M8-251 251 HP 48 km/h $152,000 190 L Heavy hauling

Recommendation: The Massey Ferguson 8S.225 strikes the best balance of cost and capability. For maximum throughput, the Fendt 728 Vario's 280 HP and 225 L fuel tank are unbeatable.

docs.title: Heavy Tractors (Over 300 HP) — Best for Biggest Fields
docs.text:
Model Horsepower Top Speed Price Fuel Capacity Best For John Deere 8R 370 370 HP 50 km/h $245,000 280 L Largest implements Case IH Steiger 370 370 HP 40 km/h $230,000 350 L Deep ripping New Holland T9.450 450 HP 40 km/h $265,000 380 L Extreme tillage Fendt 1050 Vario 517 HP 50 km/h $310,000 400 L Ultimate power Challenger MT845E 464 HP 42 km/h $275,000 360 L Tracked traction

Recommendation: The Case IH Steiger 370 offers the best value for field work. For absolute power, the Fendt 1050 Vario at 517 HP handles anything the game throws at it.

docs.text: Model Horsepower Lift Capacity Max Height Price Stoll FZ 50 (FEL) — 1,800 kg 4.0 m $4,200 Manitou MLT 625 100 HP 2,500 kg 6.5 m $35,000 JCB 435S 150 HP 3,200 kg 7.0 m $55,000

docs.title: Purchasing Tips
docs.text:
1. Match tractor power to implement requirements — every tool has a minimum HP requirement. Undersized tractors work slowly; oversized tractors waste fuel.
  2. Consider fuel efficiency — Fendt tractors generally consume less fuel per HP/hour than competitors.
  3. Speed matters — a 50 km/h tractor covers fields faster than a 40 km/h model, saving real time on large maps.
  4. Don't overbuy — start with a medium tractor (140–150 HP) and a mid-range combine. Upgrade as your operation grows.
  5. Used vehicles — check the used vehicle dealer regularly for substantial discounts on older models.
  6. Lease before you buy — leasing a vehicle for a single contract is often cheaper than purchasing for infrequent use.

Data source: Farming Simulator Wiki 1.0

docs.title: About the Game
docs.text: Fields of Mistria is a farming simulation RPG developed by NPC Studio, released in August 2024 via Early Access on Steam. Set in a charming pastoral town recovering from a mysterious earthquake, players inherit a run-down farm and work to restore both the land and the nearby village.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Details 🎮 Genre Farming Sim / RPG / Life Sim 👥 Mode Single-player 🏭 Developer NPC Studio 📅 Release August 2024 (Early Access) 🖥️ Platform PC (Steam)

docs.title: 🪄 Magic & Skills
docs.text: Unlike traditional farming sims, Fields of Mistria introduces a magic system that evolves alongside your farming skills. Players learn spells tied to seasonal elements — water magic for irrigating crops, earth magic for tilling, and fire magic for smithing. The magic system ties directly into skill progression, with higher levels unlocking more powerful enchantments for tools and crops.

docs.title: Skill Categories
docs.text:
• 🌱 Farming — Plant, water, and harvest crops; magic-enhanced yields
  • ⛏️ Mining — Explore the mines beneath Mistria, collect ores and gems
  • 🎣 Fishing — Catch freshwater and ocean fish in various biomes
  • 🌿 Foraging — Gather wild ingredients and discover rare herbs
  • 🧙 Magic — Unlock and upgrade spells through practice and quests

docs.title: 🏘️ Town & NPCs
docs.text:
Mistria is home to a cast of fully voiced NPCs with deep relationship mechanics. Each villager has daily routines, seasonal events, and personal story arcs tied to the town's recovery. Building friendships unlocks new spells, recipes, and story progression.

Tip: Spend your first few days clearing only enough land to plant a small crop patch. Use the rest of your energy exploring town and talking to villagers — early relationships unlock important tool upgrades and magic abilities faster than rushing farm expansion.

docs.text:

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
• Early game: Plant Carrots (Spring) and Onions (Summer) — cheap and fast-growing for quick cash.
  • Mid game: Invest in Strawberries (Spring) and Cranberries (Fall) — regrowth crops give sustained income.
  • Late game: Watermelons (Summer) and Pumpkins (Fall) have the highest single-harvest profit.
  • Processing: Always run high-volume crops like Tomato, Corn, and Potato through the Preserves Jar for maximum value.
  • Seed Maker: Use on expensive regrowth crops (Strawberry, Cranberry) to save money on replanting.

Data source: Fields of Mistria Wiki 1.0

docs.title: Fields of Mistria Fish Database
docs.text: Welcome to the complete fish database for Fields of Mistria. Whether you are a dedicated angler trying to complete the museum collection, a chef looking for the best ingredients, or a farmer seeking extra income, this guide covers every fish species currently available in the game. Use the tables below to find exactly where and when to catch each fish, along with their base sell prices.

docs.title: Understanding Fishing Mechanics
docs.text: Before diving into the species list, it helps to know the basics. Fishing in Fields of Mistria uses a timing-based minigame. Different fish appear in specific locations, seasons, times of day, and weather conditions. Your fishing level and the quality of your rod also affect what you can catch. Higher-quality fish (Silver, Gold, Iridium) sell for more and are worth more for cooking.

docs.title: Key Terms
docs.text:
• Location: The specific body of water or area where the fish is found.
  • Season: The in-game season(s) the fish is available.
  • Time: The in-game time window when the fish is active.
  • Weather: Some fish only appear in rain, sun, or storms.
  • Sell Price: The base price for a normal-quality fish. Prices increase with quality.

docs.title: Complete Fish List
docs.text: Below is the full database of fish organized by location. Each table includes all relevant catch conditions.

docs.title: Town River & Lake
docs.text:
The river running through the center of town and the small lake near the inn are the most accessible fishing spots for beginners.

Fish Name Season Time Weather Base Sell Price Minnow Spring, Summer, Fall Any Any 15g Carp Spring, Summer, Fall 6AM – 7PM Any 30g Perch Spring, Fall 6AM – 2PM Any 45g Sunfish Summer 9AM – 4PM Sunny 50g Catfish Summer, Fall 6PM – 2AM Rain 80g Rainbow Trout Spring 6AM – 12PM Any 65g Bass Spring, Summer, Fall Any Any 55g Pike Fall 6AM – 9AM, 4PM – 7PM Any 100g Eel Spring, Fall 6PM – 2AM Rain 70g Goldfish Spring, Summer 10AM – 4PM Sunny 120g

docs.title: Mistria Beach & Ocean
docs.text:
The ocean to the south of town offers saltwater species. You will need the Fishing Rod Upgrade from the blacksmith to cast far enough to reach deep water fish.

Fish Name Season Time Weather Base Sell Price Sardine Spring, Summer, Fall 6AM – 6PM Any 40g Herring Spring, Winter 6AM – 2PM Any 50g Flounder Spring, Summer 6AM – 7PM Any 75g Tuna Summer, Fall 10AM – 4PM Sunny 110g Red Snapper Summer, Fall 6AM – 7PM Rain 130g Halibut Spring, Fall 6AM – 11AM, 4PM – 7PM Any 150g Lobster Summer, Fall Any Any 200g Crab Spring, Summer 6AM – 12PM Any 180g Octopus Summer 6PM – 2AM Any 250g Shark Summer 6AM – 9AM, 4PM – 7PM Sunny 350g Sea Turtle Summer, Fall 10AM – 4PM Sunny 400g

docs.title: Mountain Lake
docs.text:
The lake in the northern mountain area is home to cold-water and rare species. Access requires clearing the path north of the farm.

Fish Name Season Time Weather Base Sell Price Largemouth Bass Spring, Summer, Fall 6AM – 7PM Any 60g Walleye Fall, Winter 12PM – 2AM Rain 105g Sturgeon Summer, Winter 6AM – 7PM Any 180g Salmon Fall 6AM – 7PM Any 120g Trout Spring, Summer 6AM – 12PM Any 70g Bluegill Spring, Summer 9AM – 4PM Sunny 45g Lake Catfish Summer, Fall 6PM – 2AM Any 90g Ghost Fish Winter 6PM – 2AM Any 220g Ice Carp Winter 6AM – 7PM Snow 160g Legendary Fish (The Ancient One) Spring 6AM – 9AM Rain 1,000g

docs.title: Secret Fishing Spots
docs.text:
There are a few hidden fishing locations that require specific quests or tools to access. These spots contain unique fish.

Fish Name Location Season Time Weather Base Sell Price Lava Eel Volcanic Cave (floor 10+) Any Any Any 450g Crystal Carp Crystal Caverns Any Any Any 300g Moon Jellyfish Mistria Lake (during Festival of Stars) Fall 28 8PM – 12AM Clear 500g Void Fish Sewers (unlocked after museum donation milestone) Any Any Any 600g

docs.title: Fish Uses
docs.text:
Fish are not just for selling. They have several important uses in Fields of Mistria:

  • Cooking: Many recipes require specific fish. Cooked meals provide energy, health, and temporary buffs.
  • Museum Donations: The museum curator accepts one of each species. Donating all fish unlocks special rewards.
  • Gifting: Some villagers love certain fish. For example, the fisherman NPC loves Catfish and Pike.
  • Fish Ponds: You can raise fish in ponds on your farm. They produce roe, which can be aged and sold for profit.
  • Quests: The town bulletin board occasionally requests specific fish for rewards.

docs.title: Best Fish for Profit
docs.text:
If your goal is to make gold, focus on these high-value fish:

  1. Legendary Fish (The Ancient One) – 1,000g (Spring, Rain, Mountain Lake)
  2. Moon Jellyfish – 500g (Fall 28 only)
  3. Lava Eel – 450g (Volcanic Cave)
  4. Sea Turtle – 400g (Summer/Fall, Beach)
  5. Shark – 350g (Summer, Beach)

For consistent daily income, Catfish (80g) and Red Snapper (130g) are reliable options that are easier to catch.

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
• Upgrade your rod early: Better rods let you cast farther and catch rarer fish.
  • Use bait: Craft bait from bug meat to reduce bite time and increase catch rate.
  • Check the weather: Rain and snow unlock exclusive species. Always fish during bad weather.
  • Fish at different times: Many fish are only active at dawn, dusk, or night. Set reminders to check each time window.
  • Complete the museum: The rewards for donating all fish include a permanent fishing buff and a unique decoration.
  • Cook before selling: Some fish sell for more when cooked into dishes. Check the recipe book before selling raw fish.
  • Build a fish pond: Once you have a surplus of high-value fish like Sturgeon or Lava Eel, place them in a pond for passive roe income.

docs.title: Your First Day
docs.text: When you arrive at your overgrown farm in Mistria, the first priority is survival. You'll start with basic tools — a watering can, hoe, axe, and pickaxe — and a small patch of arable land.

docs.title: Day 1 Checklist
docs.text:
• [x] Clear weeds and small debris from 6-8 farm tiles
  • [x] Visit the general store in town for starter seeds
  • [x] Plant your first crops and water them
  • [x] Meet at least 3 villagers to start building relationships
  • [x] Check the town notice board for available quests

docs.title: 🌾 First Season Strategy
docs.text: Spring (Season 1) is about building a foundation. Focus on crops with short grow times — turnips and spring greens mature in 4-5 days and provide quick gold to reinvest. Avoid large fields until you have the stamina to water them daily.

docs.title: Recommended Early Crops
docs.text: Crop Grow Time Sell Price Notes Turnip 4 days 60g Fastest turnaround Spring Green 5 days 90g Good early profit Tomato 7 days 120g Regrows after harvest

docs.title: 🧙 Unlocking Magic Early
docs.text:
Magic unlocks around day 5 after you complete your first town quest. To speed this up:

  1. Complete the "Clearing the Path" quest by removing large debris
  2. Talk to Valen, the town elder, after clearing 10 debris tiles
  3. Accept the Spell Font quest to learn your first water spell
  4. Use magic to water crops — it covers a 3×3 area and saves stamina

Tip: Always keep 200-300g in reserve for seed purchases. A common beginner mistake is spending all gold on tool upgrades before securing a steady crop income. Prioritize reinvesting in seeds until you have at least 10 planted tiles producing consistently.

docs.text:
Build relationships with the residents of Mistria! This guide covers every NPC's gift preferences, organized by Loved, Liked, Neutral, Disliked, and Hated items.

Friendship Tip: Gifting on a character's birthday gives 3× the usual friendship points. Loved gifts on birthdays are the fastest path to max hearts.

docs.title: What to do in Spring of Year 1?
docs.text:
1. ✅ Clear the farm (branches/rocks/weeds)
  2. ✅ Till 15 plots (don't overdo it, energy is limited)
  3. ✅ Plant parsnips (4-day maturity, beginner staple)
  4. ✅ Fish (early extra income)
  5. ✅ Greet NPCs (lay the foundation for friendship)

docs.title: What should I upgrade first?
docs.text: Priority Upgrade Reason 1 Backpack Limited space hurts efficiency 2 Watering Can Water more crops at once 3 Hoe Till soil faster 4 Axe Chop trees for wood 5 Pickaxe Mine for ores and stone

docs.title: Spring of Year 1
docs.text: Week Income Target Main Income Source Week 1 5K Fishing + Foraging Week 2 10K First harvest Week 3 20K Fishing upgrade Week 4 50K Strawberry Festival

docs.title: Summer Year 1 - Year 2
docs.text: Season Goal Strategy Summer 100K Mass plant blueberries Fall 200K Mass plant cranberries Winter 100K Mine ores and sell gems Year 2 500K+ Artisan goods processing

docs.title: Best-Selling Gems
docs.text: Gem Base Price How to Obtain Diamond 750G Mines floor 40+ Jade 200G Various mine floors Ruby 250G Mines floor 60+ Amethyst 150G Various mine floors Aquamarine 200G Mines floor 40/80+ Topaz 120G Various mine floors

docs.title: 🏞️ 星露谷物语新手攻略：开局第一天全指南
docs.text: 参考视频：王道長「纯新手向攻略（第一期）——开局第一天」 · 244万播放

docs.title: 📋 本篇涵盖
docs.text:
• 开局前准备：角色创建与农场类型选择
  • 第一天上午：起床、看电视、接任务
  • 第一天下午：探索农场、拜访鹈鹕镇
  • 第一天晚间：钓鱼、整理工具、存档策略
  • 新手避坑清单

docs.title: 🎮 1. 开局前的关键选择
docs.text: 刚打开游戏，主菜单下有三个关键选择：

docs.title: 1.1 农场类型
docs.text:
星露谷有 7种农场布局，新手推荐：

农场类型 推荐度 理由 标准农场 ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 最大种植面积，最适合新手熟悉游戏机制 河边农场 ⭐⭐⭐ 钓鱼方便但种地空间少 森林农场 ⭐⭐⭐⭐ 前期物资丰富（硬木/野草），喜欢采集可选 山顶农场 ⭐⭐ 地形崎岖，新手容易迷路 荒野农场 ⭐⭐ 晚上刷怪，新手压力大 四角农场 ⭐⭐⭐⭐ 联机或喜欢规整布局可选 海滩农场 ⭐ 不能放洒水器，极度不适合新手

💡 推荐：第一档玩 标准农场，熟悉后再换其他类型。

docs.title: 1.2 角色自定义
docs.text:
• 名字：随意，但建议用自己能记住的英文名（联机时方便）
  • 宠物偏好：猫🐱 和狗🐶 只是外观区别，不影响游戏性
  • 喜欢的事物：不影响游戏机制，只是NPC对话彩蛋

docs.title: 🏠 2. 第一天早上（6:00–8:00）
docs.text: 游戏从 春1日 早上6:00 开始。你站在爷爷留下的荒废农场上。

docs.title: 2.1 第一步：进屋看电视
docs.text:
进门前先按 E 打开工具栏，右键电视 可以收看：

频道 作用 建议 🌤️ 天气预报 查看明天的天气 ✅ 每天必看 — 下雨天不用浇水，适合下矿 🔮 算命占卜 查看今日运气 ✅ 每天必看 — 好运日适合下矿/钓鱼/开晶球 🍳 酱料女皇 学新菜谱（周日/周三重播） 前期不太重要 🌙 离地而居 游戏小贴士 新手建议看几期

⚡ 第一天：春1日通常是晴天，运气中等偏上。看完电视后，时间才到6:10左右，有大把时间。

docs.title: 2.2 检查信箱 📬
docs.text: 出门后在房屋右侧的邮箱处拿到第一封信——市长刘易斯 欢迎你来到星露谷，并给了你 15个防风草种子 作为见面礼。

docs.title: 3.1 开垦农田
docs.text:
先别急着去镇上！第一天最重要的任务是 开垦土地并种下防风草：

  1. 清出空地：用斧头砍几棵树（获得木材），用镰刀割杂草
  2. 耕地：用锄头右键点击土地，开垦约 15格 即可（刚好种完送的种子）
  3. 播种：打开背包（E键），选中防风草种子，在耕好的地上右键
  4. 浇水：用水壶右键浇满每一格（水壶在池塘边右键可以灌水）

📝 第一天的体力管理：你有 270体力（绿色能量条）。砍树和锄地都会消耗体力。建议保留至少一半体力去钓鱼。

docs.title: 3.2 必做：拜访罗宾（木匠）
docs.text:
往地图右侧走 → 进入深山区域 → 找到 罗宾的木匠店（营业到下午5点）。

跟她说话可以获得重要信息，同时解锁 接取任务 功能。

docs.title: 🗺️ 4. 探索鹈鹕镇
docs.text:
沿农场的路一直往下走，你会进入鹈鹕镇。第一天建议至少认识这几个关键NPC：

NPC 位置 为何重要 皮埃尔 🏪 杂货店（镇上中央） 买种子、卖作物的地方（营业到下午5点） 罗宾 🪚 木匠店（山上） 建建筑、升级工具、买家具 克林特 ⛏️ 铁匠铺（镇东南） 升级工具、开晶球 威利 🎣 鱼店（海滩） 买鱼竿、鱼饵 乔迪 / 玛妮 各自家中 前期任务会用到

⚠️ 注意：大部分商店 下午5点关门！第一天主要是认路，不用急着买东西。

docs.title: 第一天路线推荐
docs.text:
农场 → 罗宾木匠店（看路） → 皮埃尔杂货店（卖作物换钱）
→ 海滩见威利（买训练鱼竿） → 回家种地 → 傍晚钓鱼

docs.title: 🎣 5. 傍晚：钓鱼开局
docs.text: 钓鱼是 星露谷前期最赚钱的活动。

docs.title: 5.1 怎么获得鱼竿
docs.text:
如果第一天不钓鱼，可以在春3日 威利给你邮寄 一根竹鱼竿。

要提前钓鱼： - 去海滩找威利花 50g 买竹鱼竿（或 25g 训练鱼竿——鱼更难跑） - 想钓鱼需要先挖一些 虫子（用锄头挖地里的蠕虫洞），合成鱼饵

docs.title: 5.2 第一天钓鱼收益
docs.text:
时间 地点 常见鱼 售价 6:00–19:00 山区湖 虹鳟鱼/鲈鱼 75g~100g 全天 海滩 沙丁鱼 50g 全天 镇旁河 鲈鱼 50g

💡 新手钓鱼技巧：轻点鼠标控制进度条，不要一直按着。鱼动你才动，鱼停就松开让进度条回到中间。

docs.title: ⚔️ 6. 避坑清单（新手必看）
docs.text: ❌ 不要做的事 ✅ 应该做的事 ❌ 卖掉所有木材和石头 ✅ 留下至少 50个木材 修桥（春3日任务） ❌ 第一天就买很多种子 ✅ 先防风草15个，赚钱后春2日补种 ❌ 挥霍体力砍树 ✅ 保留 100+体力 钓鱼/下矿 ❌ 晚上12点后还在外面 ✅ 凌晨2点前必须上床睡觉！ ❌ 开垦超出播种能力的土地 ✅ 第一天15格就够 ❌ 把所有金币花光 ✅ 至少要留 100g 以备不时之需

docs.title: 📊 第一天目标清单
docs.text:
• [x] 起床看电视（天气预报 + 算命）
  • [x] 查看邮箱，拿到防风草种子
  • [x] 开垦15格土地，种下防风草
  • [x] 认识罗宾、皮埃尔等关键NPC
  • [x] 保留足够体力钓鱼赚钱
  • [x] 凌晨2点前回家睡觉
  • [x] 保存游戏（睡觉自动存）

docs.title: 🎯 第一天小目标总结
docs.text:
目标：把15个防风草种下去 → 认识镇上几个关键NPC → 傍晚钓鱼攒第一桶金。

春2日预告： - 皮埃尔杂货店开门（买更多防风草种子） - 如果下雨 → 不用浇水 → 适合去矿洞 - 继续钓鱼攒钱 → 春5日之前目标是 攒够2000g买草莓种子

视频来源：B站 · 王道長 — 纯新手向攻略（第一期）——开局第一天 发布于 FarmingGames.Help — 种田游戏攻略一站式数据库

docs.title: 🌾 【星露谷物语】纯新手向攻略（第二期）——春季前期钓鱼篇
docs.text: 本攻略由游戏视频教程整理生成，参考 B站 UP主 攻略视频。 使用 Hermes Agent video2guide 流水线自动创建。 最后更新: 2026-07-04

docs.title: Game Overview
docs.text:
• Developer: Singularity 6
  • Platform: PC / NS
  • Genre: Farming MMO (Free-to-Play)
  • Features: Multiplayer online farming + adventure

docs.title: Fast Leveling 1-10
docs.text:
1. Complete the tutorial → Unlock basic tools
  2. Plant wheat (3-day maturity, cooking staple)
  3. Fish → Fastest early-game money maker
  4. Mine → Important materials for tool upgrades

docs.title: Fastest Money-Making Methods
docs.text: Method Hourly Rate Requirements Fishing (hotspots) 3K-5K Fishing skill 4+ Rare bug catching 2K-4K Advanced bug net Cooking quests 1K-2K Kitchen upgrade Growing cotton 1K-3K Seeds unlocked

docs.title: Best Leveling Path
docs.text: Skill Priority Recommended Method Fishing ⭐⭐⭐ Daily hotspots Foraging ⭐⭐⭐ Pick as you go Cooking ⭐⭐ Fulfill orders for money Gardening ⭐⭐⭐ Plant every day Bug Catching ⭐ Level up when you have time Mining ⭐⭐ Go when ore respawns

docs.title: Social Features
docs.text:
• Friend System — Farm and fish together
  • Housing System — Customizable decoration
  • Festival Events — Limited-time quests
  • NPC Affinity — Give gifts to unlock story

docs.title: Beginner Pitfalls
docs.text:
1. ❌ Don't rush to expand your house — Upgrade tools first
  2. ✅ Complete NPC quests daily — Source of XP
  3. ✅ Pick up all materials on the ground — They're all useful
  4. ❌ Don't sell rare materials — You'll need them for later upgrades

docs.title: Palia — Crop Profit Guide
docs.text:
Optimize your garden layout and crop selection in Palia with this complete profit guide. Unlike most farming games, Palia uses a garden-plot grid system where crops can be planted individually or in rows, and quality (star value) affects sell prices.

Note: All prices shown are base (non-star) quality. Star-quality crops sell for roughly 1.5× base price. Gardening skill level and fertilizer can also boost yield and quality.

docs.title: Crop Profit Table
docs.text: Crop Seed Price (Gold) Growth (Hours) Harvest Yield Sell Price Profit/Plot Carrot 10 24h 1 18 8 Potato 15 48h 1 33 18 Tomato 20 48h 2 16 each 12 Onion 12 36h 1 25 13 Corn 25 60h 2 20 each 15 Rice 15 36h 1 28 13 Wheat 10 24h 1 20 10 Blueberry 30 72h 3 15 each 15 Cotton 20 48h 2 18 each 16 Pepper 18 48h 2 15 each 12 Lettuce 8 18h 1 14 6 Cabbage 22 48h 1 45 23 Bok Choy 14 30h 1 28 14 Spinach 12 24h 1 24 12 Ginger 35 72h 1 65 30 Mushroom 30 72h 2 28 each 26

docs.title: Fast Crops (24 hours or less)
docs.text: Crop Growth Profit/Plot Profit/Hour Lettuce 18h 6 0.33 Carrot 24h 8 0.33 Wheat 24h 10 0.42 Spinach 24h 12 0.50

docs.title: Medium Crops (36–48 hours)
docs.text: Crop Growth Profit/Plot Profit/Hour Onion 36h 13 0.36 Bok Choy 30h 14 0.47 Rice 36h 13 0.36 Potato 48h 18 0.38 Tomato 48h 12 0.25 Cabbage 48h 23 0.48 Cotton 48h 16 0.33 Pepper 48h 12 0.25 Corn 60h 15 0.25

docs.title: Slow Crops (72+ hours)
docs.text: Crop Growth Profit/Plot Profit/Hour Blueberry 72h 15 0.21 Ginger 72h 30 0.42 Mushroom 72h 26 0.36

docs.title: Star Quality Value Multiplier
docs.text:
Quality Price Multiplier Example: Carrot ⭐ Base (No Star) 1.0× 18 Gold ⭐⭐ Star Quality ~1.5× 27 Gold ⭐⭐⭐ Rare Star ~2.0× 36 Gold

Star quality is random but can be increased with: - Quality Up Fertilizer — Boosts star quality chance by 25% - Gardening Skill Level — Each level slightly increases base quality chance - Special Soil Plots — Some home plot layouts have natural quality bonuses

docs.title: Gardening Level Rewards
docs.text: Level Unlock 1 Basic Soil Plots (3×3), Carrot Seeds 2 Potato Seeds, Watering Can Upgrade 3 Onion Seeds, Cotton Seeds 4 Extra Soil Plot (unlock 4th row) 5 Tomato Seeds, Quality Up Fertilizer 6 Rice Seeds, Bok Choy Seeds 7 Extra Soil Plot (unlock 5th row) 8 Corn Seeds, Pepper Seeds 9 Blueberry Seeds, Mushroom Seeds 10 Ginger Seeds, Deluxe Fertilizer

docs.title: Best Crops for Each Play Style
Early Game (Levels 1–4)
docs.text: Priority Crop Why 1st Carrot Fastest turnaround, cheap seeds 2nd Wheat Quick cash + cooking ingredient 3rd Onion Decent profit, 36h growth 4th Potato Best early profit-per-plot

docs.title: Mid Game (Levels 5–8)
docs.text: Priority Crop Why 1st Cabbage Best profit-per-plot at 48h 2nd Bok Choy Fast medium profit 3rd Cotton High yield (2 per harvest) 4th Corn Good multi-harvest crop

docs.title: Late Game (Levels 9–10)
docs.text: Priority Crop Why 1st Ginger Highest profit-per-plot (30 Gold) 2nd Mushroom Second best at 26 Gold/plot 3rd Blueberry Multi-harvest, good for gifts 4th Cabbage Still top-tier for 48h cycle

docs.title: Gardening Tips
docs.text:
• Watering: Crops only grow when watered. Unwatered crops pause growth but don't die.
  • Weeds: Weeds spawn randomly on unplanted soil plots. Remove them before they spread.
  • Fertilizer: Quality Up Fertilizer is best for high-base-price crops (Ginger, Cabbage, Mushroom).
  • Multi-harvest: Tomato, Corn, Cotton, Pepper, and Blueberry produce multiple items per harvest — great for cooking ingredients.
  • Gifting: Star-quality Blueberries are a liked gift for many NPCs; Star-quality Sweet Leaf is a weekly want for some.
  • Plot expansion: Invest in extra soil plots as soon as you level up — more plots = more gold.

docs.title: Weekly Profit Estimate (20 Plots)
docs.text:
Strategy Crops Est. Gold/Week Fast Cycle Lettuce, Carrot, Spinach ~900 Balanced Cabbage, Potato, Onion, Bok Choy ~1,600 High Value Ginger, Mushroom, Blueberry ~2,200 Star Focus Ginger (Quality Fertilizer) ~3,000+

Data source: Palia Wiki 1.0

docs.title: Palia Crops Database
docs.text: Gardening is one of the most rewarding and profitable skills in Palia. Whether you are a new player looking to make your first gold or a seasoned farmer optimizing your plot for maximum efficiency, understanding each crop’s grow time, seed cost, and profit per harvest is essential. This database covers every crop currently available in Palia, organized by category, with detailed tables and practical tips to help you plan your farm.

docs.title: Crop Categories Overview
docs.text: Palia crops are divided into three main categories: Vegetables, Fruits, and Special Crops. Each category has unique growth mechanics, seed costs, and harvest yields. Vegetables are the most common and form the backbone of any farm. Fruits require more time but often yield higher profits per harvest. Special crops include rare or event-specific plants that can be used for cooking, gifting, or selling.

docs.title: Vegetable Crops
docs.text:
Vegetables are the foundation of Palia gardening. They grow quickly, have lower seed costs, and are excellent for generating consistent income. Below is the complete list of vegetable crops with their key stats.

Crop Name Seed Cost (Gold) Grow Time (Hours) Harvest Yield Sell Price per Unit Profit per Harvest Carrot 10 6 2 15 20 Potato 15 8 2 25 35 Onion 20 10 2 35 50 Tomato 25 12 3 30 65 Corn 30 14 2 50 70 Pepper 35 16 2 60 85 Rice 40 18 2 75 110 Wheat 20 8 3 20 40

Notes on Vegetables: - Carrots and Potatoes are ideal for early-game gold due to their short grow times. - Tomatoes and Corn offer the best profit per hour when planted in bulk. - Wheat is unique because it yields 3 units per harvest, making it excellent for cooking recipes.

docs.title: Fruit Crops
docs.text:
Fruits take longer to grow but produce higher-value items. Many fruits are also used in advanced cooking recipes or as gifts for villagers. Fruit crops often require trellises or specific garden layouts.

Crop Name Seed Cost (Gold) Grow Time (Hours) Harvest Yield Sell Price per Unit Profit per Harvest Blueberry 50 24 2 100 150 Apple 60 30 2 120 180 Peach 70 36 2 150 230 Cherry 80 40 2 180 280 Grape 90 48 3 130 300

Notes on Fruits: - Grapes are the most profitable fruit per harvest, but they require the longest grow time. - Blueberries are a great mid-game crop with a reasonable 24-hour grow time. - Fruits are often used in juice-making or jam recipes, which can double their value.

docs.title: Special Crops
docs.text:
Special crops include rare seeds obtained from events, quests, or high-level gardening. These crops often have unique properties, such as being used for special recipes or having a chance to produce multiple harvests.

Crop Name Seed Cost (Gold) Grow Time (Hours) Harvest Yield Sell Price per Unit Profit per Harvest Starfruit 150 72 1 500 350 Mushroom 100 48 2 200 300 Spice Sprout 80 36 2 160 240 Cotton 40 20 3 50 110

Notes on Special Crops: - Starfruit is the highest-value single crop but has a long grow time and high seed cost. It is best used for gifting or cooking. - Mushrooms are a reliable source of income for mid-to-late game players. - Cotton is essential for crafting fabric and furniture, making it valuable beyond just selling.

docs.title: Profit Analysis and Best Crops by Strategy
Best Crops for Early Game (First 20 Hours)
docs.text:
If you are just starting out, focus on crops with low seed costs and fast grow times. Carrots and Potatoes are your best friends. Plant as many as your energy allows, and reinvest your profits into more seeds.

  • Carrot: 6-hour grow time, 20 gold profit per harvest. You can plant and harvest 4 times in a single day.
  • Potato: 8-hour grow time, 35 gold profit per harvest. Slightly better profit per hour than carrots.

docs.title: Best Crops for Mid Game (20-50 Hours)
docs.text:
Once you have a steady gold flow, switch to higher-profit crops like Tomatoes, Corn, and Blueberries. These crops offer better returns per garden plot.

  • Tomato: 12-hour grow time, 65 gold profit per harvest. Great for cooking and selling.
  • Blueberry: 24-hour grow time, 150 gold profit per harvest. Perfect for daily harvest cycles.

docs.title: Best Crops for Late Game (50+ Hours)
docs.text:
Late-game players should focus on Grapes, Starfruit, and Mushrooms. These crops require patience but yield the highest profits and are essential for end-game recipes.

  • Grape: 48-hour grow time, 300 gold profit per harvest. Best profit per plot.
  • Starfruit: 72-hour grow time, 350 gold profit per harvest. Ideal for special dishes.

docs.title: Watering and Fertilizer
docs.text: All crops require regular watering. You can use a watering can or build sprinklers to automate the process. Fertilizer can be applied to reduce grow time or increase yield. Quality fertilizer reduces grow time by 20%, while Rich Fertilizer increases harvest yield by 1 unit.

docs.title: Crop Rotation and Soil Quality
docs.text: Soil quality degrades over time. Planting the same crop repeatedly reduces yield. Rotate your crops to maintain soil health. Using fertilizer also helps replenish soil nutrients.

docs.title: Multi-Harvest Crops
docs.text: Some crops, like Tomatoes and Grapes, can be harvested multiple times. After the initial harvest, the plant will regrow in half the original grow time. This makes them extremely efficient for long-term farming.

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
• Start small: Focus on Carrots and Potatoes until you have at least 500 gold.
  • Automate early: Build sprinklers as soon as possible to save time and energy.
  • Use fertilizer: Quality Fertilizer on high-value crops like Grapes can significantly boost your daily profit.
  • Sell raw or cook: Cooking ingredients into meals often doubles or triples their value. Check recipes before selling. | - Gift wisely: Some villagers love specific crops. Gifting a Starfruit to a villager can increase friendship faster than any other gift. | - Plan your layout: Leave space for trellises if you plan to grow fruits like Grapes. A well-organized garden saves time and maximizes space. | - Check the market: Crop prices fluctuate daily. Sell when prices are high for maximum profit.

docs.title: Additional Crops (Cross-Reference)
docs.text:
The following crops are available in Palia but may not appear in every seed vendor's inventory. They are unlocked through Gardening level progression or special sources.

Crop Name Seed Cost (Gold) Grow Time (Hours) Harvest Yield Sell Price per Unit Profit per Harvest Lettuce 8 18 1 14 6 Cabbage 22 48 1 45 23 Bok Choy 14 30 1 28 14 Spinach 12 24 1 24 12 Ginger 35 72 1 65 30

Notes on Additional Crops: - Lettuce is the fastest-growing crop at just 18 hours — ideal for quick turnaround plots. - Cabbage offers the best profit-per-hour among 48-hour crops. - Ginger ties with Starfruit as the highest profit-per-plot crop at 30 Gold. - Bok Choy and Spinach are solid mid-game options with moderate grow times and decent margins.

Data note: There are minor differences between community sources for some crop stats. The tables above represent the most commonly accepted values. Star-quality crops sell for ~1.5x base price.

docs.title: Early Game Combo (Gardening Lv 1-4)
docs.text:
Plot Slots Crop Mix Total Cost Est. Daily Profit Why 9 (3×3) 5 Carrot + 4 Wheat 130 ~180 Fast turnaround, cooking staples 12 (3×4) 6 Carrot + 3 Wheat + 3 Potato 165 ~260 Balanced profit and cooking use

Strategy: Plant in the evening before logging off. Carrots (6h) are ready by morning, Wheat (8h) by midday. Harvest, sell, reinvest.

docs.title: Mid Game Combo (Gardening Lv 5-8)
docs.text:
Plot Slots Crop Mix Total Cost Est. Daily Profit Why 20 (4×5) 8 Tomato + 6 Corn + 6 Cabbage 582 ~500 Multi-harvest + high profit 20 (4×5) 10 Cotton + 5 Bok Choy + 5 Rice 660 ~550 Cotton for crafting + food

Strategy: Tomatoes and Corn give you multi-harvest — plant once, harvest repeatedly. Cabbage gives the best single-harvest profit. Cotton is essential for furniture crafting.

docs.title: Late Game Combo (Gardening Lv 9-10)
docs.text:
Plot Slots Crop Mix Total Cost Est. Daily Profit Why 20 (4×5) 6 Ginger + 6 Mushroom + 8 Blueberry 810 ~1,100 Highest profit per plot 20 (4×5) 8 Grape + 6 Starfruit + 6 Spice Sprout 1,720 ~1,600 End-game max efficiency

Strategy: Ginger + Mushroom + Blueberry is your daily gold machine. Grape + Starfruit is for long-term investors (72h cycles). Use Quality Fertilizer on Ginger for star-quality bonuses.

docs.title: The "Set It and Forget It" Combo
docs.text:
For players who can only log in once per day:

Crop Why Blueberry (24h) Harvest once daily, multi-harvest Cabbage (48h) Harvest every other day, best profit/plot Tomato (12h) Can stretch to 24h without losing crop Rice (18h) Flexible timing, decent profit

Plant a 50/50 mix of Blueberries and Cabbages for ~800 Gold/day with minimal effort.

docs.title: Star Quality Optimization
docs.text:
Maximizing star-quality crops significantly boosts income:

  1. Quality Up Fertilizer on high-base-price crops (Ginger, Cabbage, Mushroom)
  2. Gardening skill levels passively increase star quality chance
  3. Special soil plots — home plot layouts with natural bonuses
  4. Sell star-quality crops individually — don't cook them unless a recipe specifically needs star ingredients

Estimated income uplift: A full plot of Ginger with Quality Fertilizer yields ~3,000+ Gold/week vs. ~1,500 without.

Note: Fishing levels unlock new rods, bait types, and zones. Higher fishing skill also increases the chance of catching rare and star-quality fish.

docs.title: Palia Fish Database — Complete Fish Catalog
docs.text: Welcome to the most comprehensive Palia fish database. Whether you’re a seasoned angler looking to complete your collection or a new player trying to finish a bundle, this guide covers every fish in the game. We’ve organized everything by location, bait type, and bundle relevance so you can spend less time guessing and more time reeling in the big ones.

docs.title: How Fishing Works in Palia
docs.text:
Fishing in Palia is a core skill that rewards you with food ingredients, quest items, and valuable resources. Each body of water has its own unique fish pool. Your success depends on three main factors:

  • Location: Rivers, lakes, ponds, and coastal waters each host different species.
  • Bait: Standard worms work for most fish, but glow worms and special bait are required for rare and epic catches.
  • Time of Day: Some fish only appear during the day (6:00–18:00), others at night (18:00–6:00), and a few are active 24/7.

You can also use fishing hooks and buffs from food to increase your catch rate, but the tables below assume no modifiers.

docs.title: Complete Fish Table by Location
docs.text: Below is the master table of every fish in Palia, sorted by the region where you can catch them. Rarity is indicated as Common (C), Uncommon (U), Rare (R), or Epic (E).

docs.title: Kilima Village — Rivers & Lakes
docs.text: Fish Name Rarity Bait Time Bundle? Silver Carp C Worm Any No Bluegill C Worm Day No Catfish U Worm Night Yes (Fisherman’s) Painted Perch U Worm Day No Kilima Sturgeon R Glow Worm Any Yes (Master Angler) Void Ray E Glow Worm Night Yes (Night Sky)

docs.title: Kilima Village — Coast & Ocean
docs.text: Fish Name Rarity Bait Time Bundle? Sardine C Worm Any No Mackerel U Worm Day No Flounder U Worm Night No Giant Stingray R Glow Worm Any Yes (Oceanic) Ancient Fish E Glow Worm Night Yes (Master Angler)

docs.title: Bahari Bay — Rivers & Lakes

docs.title: Bundle Information
docs.text: Bundles are a major endgame goal in Palia. Completing them rewards you with unique furniture, recipes, and progression items. Here’s exactly which fish you need for each bundle.

docs.title: Fisherman’s Bundle
docs.text:
• Catfish (Kilima River, Night, Worm)
  • Longnose Gar (Bahari River, Night, Glow Worm)
  • 1 additional uncommon fish of your choice

Reward: Fishing Rod Stand (decorative item)

docs.title: Oceanic Bundle
docs.text:
• Giant Stingray (Kilima Coast, Any, Glow Worm)
  • Coral Trout (Bahari Coast, Day, Glow Worm)
  • 1 additional ocean fish of uncommon or higher rarity

Reward: Seashell Chime (wall decoration)

docs.title: Night Sky Bundle
docs.text:
• Void Ray (Kilima River, Night, Glow Worm)
  • Midnight Pike (Bahari River, Night, Glow Worm)
  • Glowing Koi (Underground Lake, Night, Glow Worm)

Reward: Starry Night Lantern (light source)

docs.title: Master Angler Bundle
docs.text:
• Kilima Sturgeon (Kilima River, Any, Glow Worm)
  • Ancient Fish (Kilima Coast, Night, Glow Worm)
  • Mythic Fish (Bahari Coast, Night, Glow Worm)
  • Crystal Carp (Underground Lake, Any, Glow Worm)

Reward: Master Angler Trophy (display item) + 5,000 Gold

docs.title: Bait Guide
docs.text:
Knowing which bait to use saves you time and resources.

  • Worm Bait: Standard bait. Catches all common and uncommon fish. Can be dug from the ground or purchased from the General Store for 10 Gold each.
  • Glow Worm Bait: Required for all rare and epic fish. Crafted using 1 Worm Bait + 1 Brightshroom. Brightshrooms are found in caves and near water at night.
  • Special Bait: Some limited-time events introduce unique bait, but glow worms are your primary tool for high-tier fishing.

Pro Tip: Always carry at least 50 glow worms when heading to Bahari Bay or the Underground. Rare fish appear more frequently in these zones, and you don’t want to waste a trip.

docs.title: Fishing Skill Progression
docs.text:
Your fishing level increases as you catch fish. Higher levels unlock new recipes, better rods, and the ability to catch rarer fish without as much struggle.

Level Unlock 1 Basic Rod, Worm Bait 3 Standard Rod (longer cast) 5 Glow Worm Bait recipe 7 Fine Rod (better reel speed) 10 Exquisite Rod (best stats) 15 Fishing Hook upgrades available

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
1. Always fish at night for epics. Every epic fish in Palia (except Crystal Carp) is only available between 18:00 and 6:00. Plan your fishing trips accordingly.
  2. Use glow worms for bundles. If you’re working on bundles, skip worm bait entirely. Glow worms are the only way to catch the rare and epic fish required.
  3. Fish in the Underground for variety. The Underground Lake is the only place to catch Glowing Koi and Crystal Carp, both needed for bundles. It’s also a great spot for leveling up.
  4. Don’t sell rare fish. Before selling any fish, check if it’s needed for a bundle. You’ll save yourself hours of frustration later.
  5. Eat fishing buff food. Dishes like Fisherman’s Brew (+2 Fishing) and Spicy Fish Stew (+3 Fishing) increase your catch rate and reduce the time fish take to bite. Always cook these before a serious fishing session.
  6. Upgrade your rod first. Spend your early gold on the Standard Rod, then the Fine Rod. The longer cast and faster reel speed make a huge difference when chasing rare fish.

Happy fishing, and may your line always be tight! For more Palia guides, check out our crop database and crafting recipes.
