docs.title: Complete Friendship & Romance Guide for Palia
docs.text: Building relationships with the villagers of Palia is a core part of the game. Not only does it unlock heartwarming cutscenes and lore, but it also grants you essential recipes, furniture, and even the ability to romance multiple characters. This guide covers everything you need to know: from NPC gift preferences and weekly wants to romance levels and the fastest path to maxing out every relationship. For other Palia resources, check our Crops Guide, Fish Database, or Crop Profit Calculator.

docs.title: Beginner Tips for Friendship & Romance
docs.text:
Before diving into the specifics, here are the golden rules for efficient relationship building in Palia:

  1. Talk to Everyone Daily: Speaking to an NPC once per day gives a small friendship boost. It’s free and adds up over time.
  2. Gift Weekly Wants First: Every NPC has a "Weekly Want" that changes every Monday. Gifting this item gives a massive friendship bonus (usually 3x a normal liked gift). Prioritize these.
  3. Know Their Loves vs. Likes: "Love" gifts give the most points, "Like" gifts give moderate points, and "Dislike" gifts give nothing or negative points. Never give a disliked item.
  4. Use Cake Parties for High-Value Gifts: The best universal "Love" gifts are often high-tier cooked meals. Join a "Cake Party" (a player-organized event on the Palia Discord or in-game chat) to mass-produce cakes like Celebration Cake or Bundled Blueberry Pie with other players sharing ingredients and labor.
  5. Romance is Separate from Friendship: You can max friendship (Level 4) with everyone. Romance (Level 1-4) is an additional track that requires you to first reach friendship level 3 and then give a "Heartdrop Lily" to initiate.
  6. You Can Romance Everyone: Palia allows polyamory. You can romance every eligible NPC simultaneously without jealousy penalties.
  7. Focus on One NPC at a Time: For fastest results, focus your high-value gifts on one NPC per day until they are maxed, then move to the next.

docs.title: Friendship Levels (1-4)
docs.text:
• Level 1: Unlock basic dialogue and the ability to give gifts.
  • Level 2: Unlock the NPC's first friendship quest and a small reward (e.g., recipe, furniture).
  • Level 3: Unlock the ability to initiate romance (requires a Heartdrop Lily). Also unlocks a second friendship quest.
  • Level 4: Max friendship. Unlocks the final friendship quest and a significant reward (e.g., a rare recipe, a plushie, or a writ).

docs.title: Romance Levels (1-4)
docs.text:
• Level 1: Unlock the "Flirt" dialogue option and a pin.
  • Level 2: Unlock a romance-specific quest and a unique reward (e.g., a wall decoration).
  • Level 3: Unlock a second romance quest and a more personal reward (e.g., a portrait).
  • Level 4: Max romance. Unlocks the final romance quest and the ultimate reward (e.g., a unique outfit, a special emote, or a furniture set).

docs.title: All NPC Gift Preferences & Weekly Wants
docs.text:
Below is the master table of every NPC, their universally loved gifts, and their typical weekly want categories. Note: Weekly wants are randomized each week, but they always fall into one of the categories listed below.

NPC Universal Love (Best Gift) Universal Like (Good Gift) Weekly Want Category Auni Glow Worm, Any Fish Bugs, Foraged Items Bugs, Fish Ashura Grilled Meat, Any Sushi Meat Dishes, Mushrooms Cooked Meat Dishes Badruu Pickled Potatoes, Any Cake Vegetables, Seeds Cooked Vegetable Dishes Caleri Any Book (e.g., The Lost Chapter), Starstone Gatherables, Gems Gatherables, Books Chayne Heartdrop Lily, Any Tea Foraged Herbs, Mushrooms Foraged Items, Tea Delaila Celebration Cake, Any Pie Eggs, Milk, Butter Cooked Desserts Einar Any Fish (especially rare ones), Glow Worm Fishing Lures, Shells Fish, Fishing Supplies Elouisa Any Mushroom, Any Rare Bug Bugs, Gatherables Bugs, Mushrooms Eshe Any Gem (e.g., Emerald, Garnet), Starstone High-value Gatherables Gems, Starstones Hassian Any Meat Dish (e.g., Spicy Rice), Any Sushi Meat, Hunting Lures Cooked Meat Dishes Hekla Any Fish Dish (e.g., Fish Stew), Any Gem Fish, Gems Cooked Fish Dishes Hodari Any Sushi, Any Fish Dish Fish, Mining Resources Cooked Fish Dishes Jel Any Fabric (e.g., Silk, Cotton), Any Rare Gem Gatherables, Thread Fabric, Gems Jina Any Book, Any Gem Gatherables, Fossils Books, Gems Kenyatta Any Cake, Any Sweet (e.g., Candy) Cooked Desserts, Flowers Cooked Desserts Nai'o Any Meat Dish, Any Sushi Meat, Vegetables Cooked Meat Dishes Najuma Any Gem, Any Rare Ore (e.g., Palium) Mining Resources, Gems Gems, Ores Reth Any Cooked Meal (especially high-tier), Any Cake Ingredients, Vegetables Cooked Meals Sifuu Any Sushi, Any Meat Dish Meat, Foraged Items Cooked Meat Dishes Tamala Any Rare Flower, Any Tea Foraged Herbs, Gatherables Flowers, Tea Tau Any Meat Dish, Any Sushi Meat, Hunting Lures Cooked Meat Dishes Tish Any Furniture (especially crafted), Any Flower Gatherables, Decor Furniture, Flowers Zeki Any Gem, Any Rare Item (e.g., Plushie) Gatherables, Coins Gems, Rare Items

docs.title: Step 1: Stockpile High-Value Gifts
docs.text:
The single fastest way to gain friendship is to always give a "Love" gift. The best universal loves are:

  • Celebration Cake: Loved by almost everyone (Delaila, Kenyatta, Reth, Badruu, etc.).
  • Any Sushi: Loved by Ashura, Hassian, Hodari, Nai'o, Sifuu, Tau.
  • Any Gem (e.g., Emerald, Garnet): Loved by Eshe, Hekla, Jel, Jina, Najuma, Zeki.
  • Heartdrop Lily: Loved by Chayne and required to initiate romance.

Strategy: Focus on cooking high-tier meals. Join a Cake Party to get 50+ Celebration Cakes in one session. Alternatively, check the Palia Fish Database for rare fish locations to make sushi.

docs.title: Step 2: Prioritize Weekly Wants
docs.text:
Every Monday, check each NPC's weekly want. Gifting this item gives 3x the friendship points of a normal liked gift. If the weekly want is also a "Love" item, you get an even bigger boost.

Example: If Delaila's weekly want is "Any Pie" and you give her a Celebration Cake (which is a pie), you get a massive bonus.

docs.title: Step 3: Daily Routine
docs.text:
1. Talk to everyone (takes 5 minutes).
  2. Gift the weekly want to 2-3 NPCs per day (use your best items).
  3. Gift a "Love" item to the remaining NPCs.
  4. Complete quests as they appear. Quests give huge friendship boosts.

docs.title: Step 4: Romance Initiation
docs.text:
Once you reach friendship level 3 with an eligible NPC, you need a Heartdrop Lily to start the romance track. Heartdrop Lilies are rare flowers found in Bahari Bay (especially near the flooded steps) or purchased from the Underground Market (Zeki's secret shop) for 500 Gold each.

Tip: Farm Heartdrop Lilies by planting them in your garden. They take 5 days to grow but can be harvested repeatedly.

docs.title: Romance Level Rewards (Complete List)
docs.text:
Here is every reward you get for reaching each romance level with each NPC.

NPC Romance Level 1 Romance Level 2 Romance Level 3 Romance Level 4 Ashura Ashura's Pin "Father's Pride" Wall Decor "Father's Wisdom" Portrait Ashura's Outfit Einar Einar's Pin "Shell of Memory" Wall Decor "Einar's Catch" Portrait Einar's Emote Elouisa Elouisa's Pin "Bug Collection" Wall Decor "Elouisa's Study" Portrait Elouisa's Outfit Hassian Hassian's Pin "Hunting Trophy" Wall Decor "Hassian's Pride" Portrait Hassian's Outfit Hodari Hodari's Pin "Mining Map" Wall Decor "Hodari's Find" Portrait Hodari's Outfit Jel Jel's Pin "Fashion Sketch" Wall Decor "Jel's Masterpiece" Portrait Jel's Outfit Jina Jina's Pin "Ancient Relic" Wall Decor "Jina's Discovery" Portrait Jina's Outfit Kenyatta Kenyatta's Pin "Rebel Poster" Wall Decor "Kenyatta's Portrait" Portrait Kenyatta's Outfit Nai'o Nai'o's Pin "Farm Tools" Wall Decor "Nai'o's Harvest" Portrait Nai'o's Outfit Reth Reth's Pin "Chef's Knife" Wall Decor "Reth's Kitchen" Portrait Reth's Outfit Sifuu Sifuu's Pin "Forge Hammer" Wall Decor "Sifuu's Blade" Portrait Sifuu's Outfit Tamala Tamala's Pin "Potion Shelf" Wall Decor "Tamala's Mirror" Portrait Tamala's Outfit Tish Tish's Pin "Flower Vase" Wall Decor "Tish's Workshop" Portrait Tish's Outfit

docs.title: Advanced Strategy: The "Cake Party" Method
docs.text:
This is the most efficient way to max all relationships.

  1. Gather Ingredients: You need Sweet Leaf, Blueberry, Wheat, Egg, Milk, and Butter.
  2. Join a Cake Party: Use the Palia Discord or in-game chat to find a group. Usually, one person provides the ingredients, others provide the fuel (coal) and cooking skills.
  3. Mass Produce: You can make 50-100 Celebration Cakes in one session.
  4. Distribute: Give one Celebration Cake to every NPC who loves it (most of them). This single gift will often jump them from Level 1 to Level 2 instantly.

Pro Tip: Save your Celebration Cakes for NPCs whose weekly want is a "Cooked Dessert" for an even bigger boost.

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
docs.text:
Q: Can I lose friendship or romance progress? A: No. Once gained, friendship and romance points never decay. You cannot lose levels.

Q: How many gifts can I give per day? A: You can give one gift per NPC per day (resets at 6:00 AM Palia time).

Q: Do I need to complete quests to progress romance? A: Yes. Each romance level requires completing a specific quest given by the NPC. These quests often involve gathering rare items or cooking specific meals.

Q: Can I date multiple people at once? A: Yes! Palia has no jealousy system. You can romance every eligible NPC simultaneously.

Q: What is the fastest way to get Heartdrop Lilies? A: Plant them in your garden. They are a perennial crop, meaning they regrow after harvest. Also check Zeki's Underground Market daily.

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text:
Maxing out friendships and romances in Palia is a marathon, not a sprint, but with the right strategy, you can significantly speed up the process. Focus on weekly wants, stockpile high-value cooked meals, and talk to everyone daily. The rewards—from unique outfits to heartwarming cutscenes—are well worth the effort.

Now go out there, make some friends (and lovers), and enjoy all that Palia has to offer!

docs.title: 🏠 Cohabitation & Proudhorn Ceremony (Marriage Equivalent)
docs.text: Once you reach Romance Level 4 with an NPC, you can propose a Proudhorn Ceremony — Palia's equivalent of marriage or civil partnership.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: Requirement Detail Romance Level Level 4 with your chosen NPC Proudhorn Antler Crafted from 1 Proudhorn Antler (dropped by Proudhorn sernuks in Bahari Bay) Ceremony Location The gazebo near Kilima Lake Planning Time ~3 in-game days to prepare the ceremony

docs.title: What Cohabitation Unlocks
docs.text: Benefit Detail 🏡 Shared Housing Your partner moves into your home plot (decorates their own room) 🎁 Daily Gifts Your partner leaves gifts for you each morning 💬 Special Dialogue New romantic dialogue and interactions 👨‍👩‍👧 New Quests Cohabitation-specific quest chain 🪑 Unique Furniture Set Exclusive Proudhorn-themed furniture recipe

docs.title: Can You Cohabitate with Multiple NPCs?
docs.text: Palia allows one active cohabitation at a time. However, you can max romance with everyone — the Proudhorn Ceremony is a separate commitment step beyond Romance Level 4. You may break up and choose a different partner later.

docs.title: Breaking Up / Divorce
docs.text:
• No penalty for ending a cohabitation — friendship and romance levels remain intact.
  • Talk to your partner and choose the breakup dialogue option.
  • They move out of your house but remain friendly.

docs.title: ❌ Disliked Gifts (What NOT to Give)
docs.text:
Giving a disliked gift gives zero friendship points and wastes your daily gift opportunity. Avoid these for each NPC:

docs.title: 📆 Weekly Want System — Deep Dive
docs.text: Weekly wants update every Monday at 6:00 AM Palia time. Here's how to exploit them:

docs.title: Weekly Want Mechanics
docs.text: Factor Detail Reset Time Monday 6:00 AM (in-game time) Friendship Boost 3x normal "Like" gift points Stacking If the weekly want is also a "Love" item, the bonus multiplies further Tracking Check the "Villager" tab in the menu to see current weekly wants Notification A mail icon appears next to NPCs whose weekly want you haven't fulfilled

docs.title: Auto-Complete Checklist
docs.text:
Print this checklist and run through it every Monday:

  • [ ] Check all 23 NPCs' weekly wants in the menu
  • [ ] Check your inventory for items matching any weekly want
  • [ ] For "Cooked Meal" wants — check what ingredients you have
  • [ ] For "Fish" wants — visit the relevant fishing spot
  • [ ] For "Gatherable" wants — check Bahari Bay and Kilima
  • [ ] Prioritize NPCs whose weekly want matches their "Love" category
  • [ ] Give the weekly want gift BEFORE any other gift (it's the most efficient use)

docs.title: Weekly Want Categories Reference
docs.text: Category Examples Best Source Cooked Desserts Celebration Cake, Any Pie, Bundled Blueberry Pie Cake Party Cooked Meat Dishes Grilled Meat, Spicy Rice, Any Sushi Cooking station Cooked Fish Dishes Fish Stew, Sushi Fishing + cooking Cooked Meals (General) Any high-tier cooked meal Cooking station Gems / Starstones Emerald, Garnet, Amethyst Mining nodes Bugs Any bug (rare = more points) Bug net Fish Any fish (rare = more points) Fishing Flowers Sweet Leaf, Heartdrop Lily, rare flowers Foraging Foraged Items Mushrooms, herbs, wild plants Foraging in both zones Gatherables General gather items Pick as you explore Furniture Any crafted furniture Workbench Fabric Silk, Cotton Crafting station

Suggestion: First fish to save up $8,000 to upgrade to a wooden house, then decorate slowly. Don't build a big house right away — save money for tool upgrades.

docs.title: 🎁 Palia Redeem Codes (Active & Expired)
docs.text: Last Updated: July 5, 2026 — This page is updated as new codes are released. Bookmark this page and check back regularly for the latest Palia codes!

docs.title: How to Redeem Codes in Palia
docs.text:
1. Launch Palia and enter the game world
  2. Press ESC to open the main menu
  3. Click the "Redeem Code" button (or navigate to Settings → Redeem Code)
  4. Enter the code exactly as shown (codes are case-sensitive)
  5. Click Redeem — rewards will appear in your inventory

💡 Tip: Codes usually expire within 7–14 days, so redeem them as soon as you see them!

docs.title: ✅ Active Codes
docs.text:
Code Reward Status Added — No active codes right now ⏳ Waiting for new codes —

Palia releases codes through Twitch Drops, Discord events, and social media giveaways. Check back often!

docs.text:
• Twitch Drops — Watch Palia streams with Drops enabled to earn exclusive codes
  • Official Discord — Join the Palia Discord for announcements
  • Twitter / X — Follow @PaliaDev for code drops
  • In-Game Events — Seasonal events often include redeemable rewards
  • Newsletter — Sign up on the official Palia website

docs.title: Twitch Drop Campaigns (Active)
docs.text:
Palia frequently runs Twitch Drop campaigns instead of traditional redeem codes. To claim:

  1. Link your Palia account to Twitch at accounts.palia.com
  2. Watch any Palia streamer with Drops Enabled tag
  3. Progress bar fills as you watch (usually 1-4 hours per drop)
  4. Claim rewards on Twitch → they auto-deliver in-game

Current Twitch Drop rewards may include: - Exclusive cosmetics (outfits, gliders) - Premium currency bundles - Decor items - Nameplate designs

docs.title: ℹ️ About Redeem Codes
docs.text:
Palia is a free-to-play MMO, and its primary monetization is through the in-game shop. Redeem codes offer a way to receive:

  • Cosmetics — Outfits, gliders, accessories
  • Decor — Furniture and housing items
  • Gold & Currency — In-game gold and premium currency
  • Consumables — Boosters and utility items

Codes are typically released to celebrate milestones, holidays, or community events.

docs.title: 🏜️ My Time at Sandrock — Game Overview
About the Game
docs.text: My Time at Sandrock is an open-world crafting RPG developed by Pathea Games, set in the same universe as My Time at Portia. Released in November 2023, this sequel moves from Portia's lush valley to a harsh post-apocalyptic desert town. As a new Builder, you must revitalize Sandrock by crafting machinery, building relationships, and fending off environmental threats.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Details 🎮 Genre Crafting RPG / Life Sim 👥 Mode Single-player + Co-op 🏭 Developer Pathea Games 📅 Release November 2023 🖥️ Platform PC, PS5, Xbox, Switch

docs.title: 🔧 Workshop System
docs.text: Unlike traditional farming sims, Sandrock revolves around your Workshop — a crafting-based business. You accept commissions from townsfolk, build machines like furnaces and grinders, and manufacture everything from simple nails to advanced engines. Your workshop rank determines access to better blueprints and higher-paying commissions.

docs.title: Core Workshop Machines
docs.text: Machine Materials Needed Purpose ⌛ Furnace Copper ore + Stone Smelt basic bars ⚙️ Grinder Wood + Stone Process sand and ores 🔩 Assembly Station Iron bars + Circuits Build complex items 🪚 Worktable Wood Craft starter parts

docs.title: 🏙️ Desert Setting & Exploration
docs.text:
Sandrock's harsh desert environment is central to the experience. Water is scarce and must be managed carefully — you'll need a dew collector for hydration and a reliable supply chain for materials. The world is filled with ancient ruins, hazardous ruins for combat-based loot runs, and scattered oases that serve as rest points.

Tip: In the first week, focus entirely on filling commissions for the town board rather than hoarding materials. Each successful commission raises your workshop rank and unlocks better blueprints exponentially faster than free-building. Accept every commission you can physically fulfill.

docs.title: 🔧 My Time at Sandrock — Workshop Starter Guide
Setting Up Your Workshop
docs.text: Your journey begins with a dilapidated workshop and a stack of debts. Your first task is to convert scrap metal and old wood into functional machinery. The tutorial walks you through building your first Worktable and Stone Furnace — these are your lifelines for the first week.

docs.title: Initial Build Order
docs.text: Priority Item Why 1 Worktable Enables basic crafting 2 Stone Furnace Smelt copper for tool upgrades 3 Dew Collector Passive water generation 4 Wooden Chest Extra storage for materials

docs.title: 💰 Earning Gols (Money)
docs.text: The primary income in Sandrock comes from commissions. Check the Commerce Commission Board daily for available jobs. Early commissions typically ask for basic items like copper bars, wooden sticks, or simple mechanisms.

docs.title: Best Early Money Methods
docs.text: Method Gols/Day Risk 📋 Town Commissions 200-800 Low — guaranteed payment 🏚️ Ruins Salvage 300-600 Medium — combat required 🌵 Foraging (Cactus, Sandcoral) 100-300 Low — free resources ⛏️ Mining + Selling Excess Ore 150-400 Low — always profitable

docs.title: 🛠️ Tool Upgrades
docs.text:
Your tools — pickaxe, axe, and hoe — can be upgraded at the Blacksmith. Each upgrade level increases efficiency dramatically.

Level Cost Benefit Copper 20 Copper bars 2x gather speed Iron 30 Iron bars 2x gather speed, can break desert rocks Stainless 50 Stainless bars 3x gather speed + larger area

Tip: Always keep at least 5 bronze bars in your inventory for emergency commissions. Many early board requests are time-sensitive and missing a deadline hurts your reputation. If you're short on materials, the scrap piles around town respawn daily and are completely free to salvage.

docs.title: My Time at Sandrock — NPC Gift Guide
docs.text: Building friendships with Sandrock's quirky residents unlocks discounts, workshop perks, romance options, and story progression. Each character has distinct tastes across five gift tiers. Prioritize Loved gifts, especially on birthdays, for maximum relationship gain.

docs.title: Gift Giving Tips
docs.text:
• Loved gifts give the highest relationship boost — always check an NPC's Loved items before visiting.
  • Birthday gifts provide a massive 3x multiplier on relationship points.
  • Item quality matters — higher-quality versions of Loved items grant additional points.
  • Weekly limits apply: you can give each NPC up to 2 gifts per week (1 for spouses).
  • Commission rewards sometimes include exclusive Loved gifts for specific NPCs.

Data source: My Time at Sandrock Wiki 1.0

docs.title: My Time at Sandrock — Resource & Material Guide
docs.text: Surviving and thriving in the harsh desert of Sandrock depends on knowing where to find every resource, how to gather it efficiently, and what it's used for. This comprehensive guide covers all major materials organized by type.

docs.title: Water & Fuel
docs.text:
Water is the most critical resource in Sandrock. It is used for farming, crafting, and hydration.

Resource Source Primary Use Dew Drops Grass, Early Morning Harvest Minimal Water, Early Farming Water Bottle Water Tower, Purchase Farming, Cooking Large Water Tank Water Tower (upgraded) Bulk Water Storage Portable Water Tank Purchase from Water World Water on the Go Wood Chopping Trees Furnace Fuel Hardwood Chopping Large Trees Efficient Furnace Fuel Coal Mining (Gecko Station+), Purchase High-Efficiency Fuel Power Stones Mining, Monster Drops Machine Power Source

docs.title: Gathering Efficiency Tips
docs.text:
1. Upgrade your pickaxe and axe as soon as possible — higher tiers unlock access to rarer ores and woods.
  2. Scavenge the Breach daily for early-game scrap and data disks — it refreshes each in-game day.
  3. Plant Sandrice and Cactus on your farm for a renewable source of plant materials.
  4. Save Titanium and Magnesium ores for alloy production rather than selling raw — refined materials are worth significantly more.
  5. Check the train that passes through Sandrock — sometimes it drops rare materials on the tracks.
  6. Commission board rewards often include rare materials that are difficult to mine — prioritize these commissions.
  7. Geode processing at the Grinder station yields gems and rare crafting components — always crack geodes open rather than selling them.

Data source: My Time at Sandrock Wiki 1.0

docs.title: 🌾 Stardew Valley Complete Game Guide
docs.text:
星露谷物语（Stardew Valley） 是一款经典的牧场经营 RPG 游戏。本攻略站收录了从新手入门到全成就解锁的完整指南，涵盖 1.6 更新的全部新内容。

数据来源：Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6 | 持续更新中

docs.title: 📋 攻略目录总览
docs.text:
本攻略站共收录 30+ 个攻略页面，按以下类别组织。点击页面名即可跳转。

docs.title: 🐄 Animal & Ranching Profit Guide
docs.text:
Data version: Stardew Valley 1.6 | Complete animal products, breeding, profitability, and ranching strategies

Animals are one of the most reliable income sources in Stardew Valley. This guide covers all farmable animals, their products, upgrade paths, breeding, and profit optimization.

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6

docs.title: 🐔 Chickens
docs.text: || Item | Cost | Details | |:---|:---|:---| | Coop | 4,000g + 250 Wood | Holds 4 chickens | | Large Coop | 6,000g + 350 Wood | Holds 8 chickens + auto-feeder | | Chicken (Rooster/Hen) | 800g | Buy from Marnie's Ranch | | Egg | Free (daily) | Hen produces 1 egg/day when happy | | Large Egg | Free (daily) | Happy chicken produces large egg | | Gold Large Egg | Free (weekly) | Very happy chicken (10 hearts) |

docs.title: Product Values
docs.text: Product Base Price Large Price Gold Price Processing Mayonnaise 50g → 190g 80g → 304g 100g → 380g 3.8x markup Dinosaur Mayonnaise — — — N/A (duck only)

docs.title: Daily Profit (Per Chicken)
docs.text: Scenario Daily Income Weekly Income Sad chicken (regular egg) 50g/day 350g Happy chicken (large egg) 80g/day 560g Very happy (gold egg) 100g/week 100g Mayonnaise machine (large egg) 304g/day 2,128g

docs.title: 🦆 Ducks
docs.text: || Item | Cost | Details | |:---|:---|:---| | Duck | 1,600g | Buy from Marnie's Ranch (requires coop) | | Duck Egg | 95g | Larger than chicken egg | | Large Duck Egg | 140g | Happy duck | | Gold Duck Egg | 175g | Very happy duck |

docs.title: Product Values
docs.text: Product Base Price Large Price Gold Price Processing Duck Mayonnaise 95g → 275g 140g → 405g 175g → 508g 2.9x markup

docs.title: Daily Profit (Per Duck)
docs.text:
Scenario Daily Income Weekly Income Sad duck (regular egg) 95g/day 665g Happy duck (large egg) 140g/day 980g Mayonnaise machine (large) 405g/day 2,835g

Ducks can swim in the pond — this makes them slightly happier and more productive. They also produce eggs even in winter (unlike chickens in older versions, though 1.6 fixed this).

docs.text: || Item | Cost | Details | |:---|:---|:---| | Barn | 6,000g + 350 Wood | Holds 5 cows | | Large Barn | 12,000g + 400 Wood | Holds 8 cows + auto-feeder | | Cow | 2,000g | Buy from Marnie's Ranch | | Milk | 190g | Produced daily when happy | | Large Milk | 225g | Happy cow |

docs.text: Product Base Price Large Price Processing Cheese 190g → 230g 225g → 274g 1.2x markup Gold Cheese — — 300g

docs.title: Daily Profit (Per Cow)
docs.text: Scenario Daily Income Weekly Income Sad cow (milk) 190g/day 1,330g Happy cow (large milk) 225g/day 1,575g Cheese press (large milk) 274g/day 1,918g Gold water can (cheese) 300g/day 2,100g

docs.title: 🐐 Goats
docs.text: || Item | Cost | Details | |:---|:---|:---| | Goat | 2,500g | Buy from Marnie's Ranch | | Goat Milk | 225g | Produced daily when happy | | Large Goat Milk | 275g | Happy goat |

docs.title: Product Values
docs.text: Product Base Price Large Price Processing Goat Cheese 225g → 375g 275g → 459g 1.7x markup Gold Goat Cheese — — 625g

docs.title: Daily Profit (Per Goat)
docs.text:
Scenario Daily Income Weekly Income Sad goat (milk) 225g/day 1,575g Happy goat (large milk) 275g/day 1,925g Cheese press (large) 459g/day 3,213g Gold water can (cheese) 625g/day 4,375g

Goats are the most profitable animal per unit when kegged/cheesed. The higher base price of goat milk translates to significantly higher goat cheese profits.

docs.title: 🐑 Sheep
docs.text: || Item | Cost | Details | |:---|:---|:---| | Sheep | 1,600g | Buy from Marnie's Ranch | | Wool | 340g | Shorn every 4 days when happy | | Gold Wool | 470g | Very happy sheep |

docs.title: Product Values
docs.text: Product Base Price Gold Price Processing Cloth 340g → 470g 470g → 651g 1.4x markup

docs.title: Daily Profit (Per Sheep)
docs.text:
Scenario Income (per 4 days) Daily Average Sad sheep (wool) 340g 85g/day Happy sheep (wool) 470g 118g/day Cloth press (happy) 651g 163g/day

Sheep are the lowest-return animal due to the 4-day shearing interval and modest cloth markup. Only keep sheep if you need wool for quests or the Deer Scanner.

docs.title: 🐷 Pigs
docs.text: || Item | Cost | Details | |:---|:---|:---| | Pig | 1,600g | Buy from Marnie's Ranch (requires barn) | | Truffle | 290g | Found outdoors in Fall (any tree shade) | | Large Truffle | 360g | Very happy pig | | Gold Truffle | 500g | Max happiness pig |

docs.title: Product Values
docs.text: Product Base Price Processing Preserves Jar (Truffle Jelly) 290g → 725g 2.5x markup Gold Truffle (jelly) 500g → 1,250g 2.5x markup

docs.title: Daily Profit (Per Pig)
docs.text:
Scenario Fall Income (per 7 days) Daily Average Happy pig (truffle) ~290g × 7 290g/day Preserves jar (truffle) ~725g × 7 725g/day Very happy (large truffle) ~360g × 7 360g/day Max happy (gold truffle) ~500g × 7 500g/day Jelly (gold truffle) ~1,250g × 7 1,250g/day

Pigs are the highest single-animal profit in Fall. A happy pig finds 1 truffle per day under trees. With preservers jar, that's 725g/day per pig. No other animal comes close in Fall.

docs.title: 🐝 Bees
docs.text: || Item | Cost | Details | |:---|:---|:---| | Bee Hive | 2,500g + 100 Wood | Buy from Willy or craft | | Big Bee Hive | 4,000g + 100 Wood | Buy from Willy or craft | | Honey | 150g | Produced every 4 days | | Large Honey | 200g | Happy bees | | Gold Honey | 250g | Very happy bees | | Bee Box (Wild Honey) | Free | Place near wild flowers |

docs.title: Product Values
docs.text: Product Base Price Processing Jelly (honey) 150g → 375g 2.5x markup Gold Honey (jelly) 250g → 625g 2.5x markup Bee House + Fairy Rose — Truffle Oil 180g

docs.title: Daily Profit (Per Bee Hive)
docs.text:
Scenario Income (per 4 days) Daily Average Sad bees (honey) 150g 37.5g/day Happy bees (large honey) 200g 50g/day Jelly (happy bees) 500g 125g/day Gold honey jelly 625g 156g/day

Bees need flowers nearby to produce honey. Plant fairy roses, poppies, or spring flowers around hives for maximum output. Truffle Oil from bee houses + fairy roses sells for 180g — excellent passive income.

docs.title: 🐛 Slimes
docs.text: || Item | Cost | Details | |:---|:---|:---| | Slime Egg (Green) | Free | Slime Hutch产出 | | Slime Egg (Blue) | Free | Slime Hutch产出 | | Slime Egg (Purple) | Free | Slime Hutch产出 | | Slime Egg (Gold) | Free | Slime Hutch产出 | | Slime Egg (Ocean) | Free | Slime Hutch产出 | | Slime Hutch | 10,00g + 200 Stone + 100 Wood | Dedicated building |

docs.title: Product Values
docs.text:
Product Base Price Processing Green Slime Egg 50g — Blue Slime Egg 100g — Purple Slime Egg 200g — Gold Slime Egg 400g — Ocean Slime Egg 150g — Slime Free Drop from slimes Slime Egg (incubated) 50-400g Sell or breed

Slime Hutch on Ginger Island lets you breed slimes for egg sales. Gold slimes produce the most valuable eggs.

docs.title: 🐕 Rabbits
docs.text: || Item | Cost | Details | |:---|:---|:---| | Rabbit | 1,600g | Buy from Marnie's Ranch (requires large coop) | | Rabbit's Wool | 420g | Collected every 4 days | | Lucky Rabbit's Foot | Free | Rare drop from rabbits |

docs.title: Product Values
docs.text:
Product Base Price Processing Cloth (rabbit wool) 420g → 470g 1.1x markup

Rabbits have poor profit ROI — the 4-day cycle and minimal cloth markup make them less profitable than other animals. Keep them for the Lucky Rabbit's Foot (quest item) or if you have excess coop space.

docs.title: 🐕 Dinosaur
docs.text: || Item | Cost | Details | |:---|:---|:---| | Dinosaur Egg | 500g (Traveling Cart) or hatch from geode | Rare find | | Dinosaur Mayonnaise | 350g → 800g | 2.3x markup | | Dinosaur Wool | — | — |

docs.title: Daily Profit (Per Dinosaur)
docs.text:
Scenario Daily Income Mayonnaise machine 800g/day

Dinosaurs produce mayonnaise daily — the highest daily artisan product from any animal. The 500g seed cost pays back in less than 1 day of mayonnaise production.

docs.title: 📊 Animal Profit Comparison
docs.text: || Animal | Product | Base Price | Processed Price | Daily Profit | Weekly Profit | |:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---| | Pig (Fall) | Truffle Jelly | 725g | 725g | 725g | 5,075g | | Pig (Fall) | Gold Truffle Jelly | 1,250g | 1,250g | 1,250g | 8,750g | | Dinosaur | Dinosaur Mayo | 800g | 800g | 800g | 5,600g | | Goat | Goat Cheese | 459g | 459g | 459g | 3,213g | | Cow | Cheese | 274g | 274g | 274g | 1,918g | | Duck | Duck Mayo | 405g | 405g | 405g | 2,835g | | Chicken | Mayonnaise | 304g | 304g | 304g | 2,128g | | Sheep | Cloth | 163g | 163g | 163g | 1,141g | | Bee Hive | Honey Jelly | 156g | 156g | 156g | 1,092g | | Rabbit | Cloth | 118g | 118g | 118g | 826g |

docs.title: Early Game (Year 1, Spring-Summer)
docs.text:
Priority: Chickens → Mayonnaise Machine
Investment: 800g chicken + 4,000g coop + 4,000g machine
Daily Income: 304g/day per chicken
Strategy: Start with 4 chickens, max out mayonnaise production

docs.title: Mid Game (Year 1, Fall-Winter)
docs.text:
Priority: Add Pigs (Fall only) + Goats
Investment: 1,600g pig + 2,500g goat
Daily Income: 725g/day (pig) + 459g/day (goat cheese)
Strategy: Maximize Fall pig profits, transition to goats in Winter

docs.title: Late Game (Year 2+)
docs.text:
Priority: Dinosaurs + Bee Houses + Truffle Oil
Investment: 500g dinosaur egg + 2,500g bee hives
Daily Income: 800g/day (dino) + 156g/day (bee) + 725g/day (pig Fall)
Strategy: Diversify. Dinosaurs for steady income, pigs for Fall spikes

docs.title: 🔄 Animal Happiness System
docs.text: Happiness Indicator Effect 0 hearts 😢 Sad Regular products, may refuse to leave coop/barn 1-4 hearts 😐 Unhappy Small chance of large products 5-7 hearts 🙂 Happy Large products guaranteed, leaves building automatically 8-9 hearts 😊 Very Happy Gold products possible 10 hearts ❤️ Max Happy Gold products guaranteed, special events

docs.title: How to Increase Happiness
docs.text: || Action | Hearts Gained | |:---|:---| | Pet animal daily | +1 heart/day (up to 10) | | Auto-petter machine | +0.5 hearts/day | | Large Auto-Petter | +1 hearts/day | | Leave animal outside (chickens/ducks) | +1 heart/day (good weather) | | Feed hay in winter | Prevents happiness loss | | Rain/snow (coop animals inside) | No penalty |

docs.title: Auto-Petter Comparison
docs.text: || Machine | Cost | Effect | |:---|:---|:---| | Auto-Petter | 10,000g | Pets 1 animal every few minutes | | Large Auto-Petter | 20,000g | Pets ALL animals every few minutes |

docs.title: 💡 Ranching Tips
docs.text:
1. Pigs in Fall are unbeatable — Place them near trees, let them roam, collect truffles daily. One pig can earn 725-1,250g/day.
  2. Goat cheese > Cow cheese — The 1.7x markup on goat milk beats the 1.2x on cow milk. Prioritize goats once you can afford them.
  3. Dinosaur mayonnaise is the best daily artisan product — 800g/day with no inputs after purchase. ROI in less than 1 day.
  4. Auto-petter is worth it for large ranches — At 8+ animals, the time saved petting adds up to hours per week.
  5. Bee hives near flowers — Place hives next to fairy roses, poppies, or spring flowers for maximum honey output.
  6. Rabbits are niche — Only keep for Lucky Rabbit's Foot quests. Poor ROI compared to chickens.
  7. Sheep are the worst animal — 4-day shearing cycle + low cloth markup. Skip unless you need wool.
  8. Gold Watering Can for animals — Increases chance of gold-quality products. Essential for max profits.

All prices and values are based on Stardew Valley 1.6.9. Animal products may vary slightly with professions (Coopmaster gives +20% chicken products, Rancher gives +25% animal products).

docs.title: How to Spend Your Money: Mid-to-Late Game Economic Chain
docs.text:
Year 1: Fish for money → Buy seeds → Plant Strawberries (Spring 13)
Year 2: Greenhouse Starfruit + Keg → 10k+/day
Year 3: Iridium quality automation → Ginger Island full planting → 50k+/day

💡 Most Important Rule: Once you unlock the Keg, build 50-100 Kegs as fast as possible. Your earning rate will directly increase 10x.

docs.title: 👢 Complete Boots & Footwear Guide
docs.text:
Boots are an essential equipment slot that provides defense (reduces damage taken) and immunity (reduces debuff duration). This guide covers every pair of boots available in Stardew Valley 1.6, from the humble Sneakers to the endgame Cinderclown Shoes.

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6

docs.title: 📊 Boots Comparison Table
docs.text: Boots Defense Immunity How to Get Sell Price Rating Sneakers +1 −1 Starting equipment 50g ⭐⭐ Rubber Boots +1 −1 Mines F10 reward (chest) 50g ⭐⭐ Leather Boots +1 −1 Adventurer's Guild (500g) 100g ⭐⭐ Work Boots +1 −1 Adventurer's Guild (500g) 100g ⭐⭐ Combat Boots +3 −1 Adventurer's Guild (1,500g) 250g ⭐⭐⭐ Tundra Boots +2 −1 Adventurer's Guild (750g) 150g ⭐⭐ Thermal Boots +1 +1 Adventurer's Guild (1,000g) 125g ⭐⭐⭐ Dark Boots +2 −1 Adventurer's Guild (2,000g) 300g ⭐⭐⭐ Firewalker Boots +3 +3 Adventurer's Guild (4,000g) 500g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Genie Shoes +1 −1 Adventurer's Guild (2,500g) 250g ⭐⭐ Cowboy Boots +3 −1 Adventurer's Guild (5,000g) 450g ⭐⭐⭐ Darker Boots +4 −1 Adventurer's Guild (6,000g) 600g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Crystal Shoes +3 +5 Adventurer's Guild (10,000g) 750g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Mermaid Boots +5 +5 Adventurer's Guild (20,000g) 1,200g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Dragonscale Boots +7 +7 Adventurer's Guild (25,000g) 1,500g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Space Boots +4 +4 Adventurer's Guild (15,000g) 750g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Leprechaun Shoes +1 +1 Adventurer's Guild (3,000g) 200g ⭐⭐ Cinderclown Shoes +6 +6 Combat Mastery (1.6) 1,600g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

docs.title: Early Game (Years 1–2)
docs.text: Boots Why Get It Strategy Leather Boots First upgrade from Sneakers Buy at Adventurer's Guild for 500g — cheap defense boost Combat Boots +3 defense for the mid-Mines Best value upgrade at 1,500g — carry to floor 80+ Thermal Boots +1 immunity against slime debuffs Good for Slime Hutch and Quarry Mine runs

docs.title: Mid Game (Year 2–3)
docs.text: Boots Why Get It Strategy Firewalker Boots +3 defense & immunity Excellent all-rounder for Skull Cavern early runs Crystal Shoes +3 defense, +5 immunity Best mid-game boots — immunity to most debuffs Space Boots +4 defense & immunity Solid stats, reasonable price (15,000g)

docs.title: Late Game (Year 3+)
docs.text: Boots Why Get It Strategy Dragonscale Boots +7 defense & immunity Second-best in game — 25,000g Cinderclown Shoes +6 defense & immunity Free (Combat Mastery) — requires Mastery Cave in Cindersap Mermaid Boots +5 defense & immunity Budget alternative at 20,000g

docs.title: 📊 Boots Stat Evolution
docs.text:
This chart shows how defense and immunity improve as you progress:

Defense:  Sneakers (1) → Combat (3) → Firewalker (3) → Crystal (3) → Space (4) → Cinderclown (6) → Dragonscale (7)
Immunity: Sneakers (−1) → Thermal (+1) → Firewalker (+3) → Space (+4) → Crystal (+5) → Cinderclown (+6) → Dragonscale (+7)

Key insight: Immunity is more valuable than defense in the late game. Debuffs from serpents, slimes, and ghosts are the main cause of death — not raw damage.

docs.title: 🏆 Boot Progression Roadmap
docs.text:
1. Day 1: Sneakers (+1/−1) — all you need for the first mines run
  2. Floor 10: Check for Rubber Boots in the chest — free upgrade
  3. Guild unlock (Slime kills x10): Buy Combat Boots (1,500g) — best early investment
  4. After reaching floor 80: Save for Firewalker Boots (4,000g)
  5. Deeper Mines clear: Save 15,000g for Space Boots or 20,000g for Mermaid Boots
  6. Mastery Cave: Claim free Cinderclown Shoes
  7. Endgame: Dragonscale Boots (25,000g) — final pair, only if you have gold to spare

docs.title: 💡 Tips
docs.text:
• Boots never break — buy the best you can afford, they last forever
  • Cinderclown Shoes are the only boots that don't cost money (Combat Mastery reward)
  • Immunity reduces the duration of: Slime debuff (slow), Ghost debuff (dark), Bat debuff (speed down), and Serpent debuff (defense down)
  • Defense reduces damage by roughly 1 HP per point, so +7 defense = 7 less damage per hit
  • You can find rare boot drops in Volcano Dungeon treasure rooms — always check them

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6. All boot stats, prices, and locations verified for version 1.6.9.

docs.title: 💡 Crafting Tips
docs.text:
1. Keg is king — Always prioritize kegs over preserves jars. Wine profit is 2-3x jelly.
  2. Seed Maker for Ancient Fruit — The single best investment after your first ancient seed. Turns one seed into an infinite income stream.
  3. Beer has the highest multiplier — Wheat grows in 4 days, costs 10g, and beer sells for 200g. That's a 20x return.
  4. Mayonnaise is the best early-game income — Chickens are cheap, eggs are free, and mayo sells for 190g.
  5. Crystalarium for gems — Dump duplicate diamonds/emeralds into a crystalarium for passive gem income.
  6. Super Bomb for mining — 5x faster than a pickaxe for clearing floors. Essential for Skull Cavern.
  7. Gold Watering Can — Buy from the Wizard after donating the first prismatic shard. Guarantees iridium-quality crops.
  8. Repair Kits — Always carry basic repair kits when going deep into mines. A broken pickaxe = game over.

All recipes and costs are based on Stardew Valley 1.6.9. Crafting recipes unlock at Farming Level 6. Some items have different costs or requirements in multiplayer.

docs.title: Top 5 Keg / Preserves Profit
docs.text:
Crop Base Price Product Product Price Multiplier Starfruit ⭐ 750g Starfruit Wine 🍷 2250g 3x Melon 🍈 250g Melon Wine 🍷 750g 3x Pumpkin 🎃 320g Pumpkin Juice 🧃 720g 2.25x Hops 🌿 25g Pale Ale 🍺 300g 12x Wheat 🌾 10g Beer 🍺 200g 20x

Hops are the Keg efficiency king! Though the base price is low, they're harvested daily, take 11 days to mature, then produce every day — a year-round Keg assembly line.

Note: Fruits go into Kegs to make Wine (3x base price). Vegetables go into Kegs to make Juice (2.25x base price). Pumpkin is a vegetable, so it produces Pumpkin Juice, not wine. Blueberries (fruit) produce Blueberry Wine at 150g (50g × 3).

docs.title: ⚙️ Equipment & Tool Upgrade Guide
docs.text:
Data version: Stardew Valley 1.6 | Complete upgrade paths, costs, materials, and comparisons

Every tool in Stardew Valley can be upgraded to improve its performance. This guide covers all 5 tools (Pickaxe, Axe, Sword, Watering Can, Hoe), the upgrade costs, materials required, and performance comparisons.

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6

docs.title: 🪓 Pickaxe Upgrades
docs.text:
The pickaxe determines how many tiles you can break at once and how quickly you can mine.

Upgrade Material Required Cost Effect Rating Copper Copper Bar ×5 2,000g Breaks 1 tile, slightly faster swing ⭐⭐ Iron Iron Bar ×5 5,000g Breaks 1 tile, faster swing, wider range ⭐⭐⭐ Gold Gold Bar ×5 10,000g Breaks 1 tile, even faster, slight range increase ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Iridium Iridium Bar ×5 25,000g Breaks 1 tile, fastest swing, best durability ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Upgrade Cost Breakdown: - Copper → Iron: 2,000g + 5 Copper Bars (500g ore) = 2,500g total - Iron → Gold: 5,000g + 5 Gold Bars (1,250g ore) = 6,250g total - Gold → Iridium: 25,000g + 5 Iridium Bars (750g ore) = 25,750g total - Grand Total (Copper → Iridium): ~34,500g + all ores

Performance Comparison:

Upgrade Swing Speed Durability Special Copper 1.0x 50 hits Base tool Iron 1.2x 100 hits Slightly faster Gold 1.4x 200 hits Noticeably faster Iridium 1.6x 999 hits Near-infinite

docs.title: 🪓 Axe Upgrades
docs.text:
The axe determines chopping speed and whether you can chop larger trees.

Upgrade Material Required Cost Effect Rating Copper Copper Bar ×5 2,000g Chops regular trees (green stump) ⭐⭐ Iron Iron Bar ×5 5,000g Chops regular + large trees (brown stump) ⭐⭐⭐ Gold Gold Bar ×5 10,000g Faster chop, chops hardwood trees (purple stump) ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Iridium Iridium Bar ×5 25,000g Fastest chop, chops all tree types ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Key Milestone: Iron axe is the minimum needed to chop large trees (brown stumps), which yield 2 hardwood. Gold axe adds hardwood trees (purple stumps).

Chop Speed Comparison:

Upgrade Chops per Tree (Regular) Chops per Tree (Large) Chops per Tree (Hardwood) Copper 4 Cannot chop Cannot chop Iron 3 4 Cannot chop Gold 2 3 4 Iridium 1 2 3

docs.title: ⚔️ Sword Upgrades
docs.text:
Swords are upgraded at the Adventurer's Guild (not Clint's). They determine combat damage.

docs.title: 💧 Watering Can Upgrades
docs.text:
Watering can upgrades determine how many tiles you water at once.

Upgrade Material Required Cost Tiles Watered Rating Copper Copper Bar ×5 2,000g 1 tile (cross) ⭐⭐ Iron Iron Bar ×5 5,000g 4 tiles (2x2) ⭐⭐⭐ Gold Gold Bar ×5 10,000g 9 tiles (3x3) ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Iridium Iridium Bar ×5 25,000g 25 tiles (5x5) ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Time Savings Analysis (per 120-tile farm):

Upgrade Waterings Needed Time Saved vs Copper Copper 120 waterings — — Iron 30 waterings 75% ~90 min/day Gold 13–14 waterings 89% ~105 min/day Iridium 5 waterings 96% ~115 min/day

Recommendation: Gold watering can is the sweet spot for most players. The jump from 4 tiles to 9 tiles cuts waterings by more than half. Iridium is nice but the marginal gain from 9 to 25 tiles is smaller.

docs.title: 📊 Upgrade Priority Guide
docs.text: Priority What to Upgrade When P0 Watering Can → Gold Year 1, Spring Biggest time savings early P0 Axe → Iron Year 1, immediately Required for large trees P1 Pickaxe → Iron Year 1, Spring Better mining speed P1 Sword → Cutlass Year 1, after reaching Mine F40 Survive deeper mines P2 Watering Can → Iridium Year 2 Maximum convenience P2 Hoe → Gold Year 2 Large farm efficiency P3 Pickaxe → Gold Year 2 Faster mining P3 Axe → Gold Year 2 Chop hardwood trees P4 Pickaxe → Iridium Year 3+ Max mining speed P4 Axe → Iridium Year 3+ Max chopping speed P4 Hoe → Iridium Year 3+ Max tilling speed P4 Sword → Lava Katana Year 3+ Best pre-Galaxy sword

docs.title: 💡 Pro Tips
docs.text:
1. Upgrade the watering can first — it saves the most time daily. Gold (9 tiles) is the sweet spot.
  2. Iron axe ASAP — you need it to chop large trees for hardwood, essential for barns/coops.
  3. Don't rush Iridium tools — Gold tools are sufficient for 90% of gameplay. Save iridium for weapons.
  4. Keep a backup Copper pickaxe — if yours breaks (rare), you can still mine.
  5. Upgrade at the Blacksmith — Clint handles all tool upgrades. Talk to him, select your tool.
  6. The Sword upgrade path goes through the Adventurer's Guild — not Clint's shop. Talk to Willy's replacement.
  7. Iridium tool durability is effectively infinite — 999 hits means you'll never need to upgrade again.

This guide covers all standard tool and building upgrades in Stardew Valley 1.6. Prices and costs reflect base values without any discounts from professions or events.

docs.title: 🍁 Complete Fall Guide
docs.text: Fall is the most profitable season in Stardew Valley! Cranberries provide continuous yield + Pumpkins sell at a high price, combined with keg profits to double your income.

docs.title: Fall Crop Recommendations
docs.text:
Crop Price Days to Mature Regrowth Season Income Rating Cranberries 🫐 75g/2 berries 7 days Every 2 days Highest ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Pumpkin 🎃 320g 13 days One-time High ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Amaranth 🌿 150g 7 days One-time Medium ⭐⭐ Eggplant 🍆 60g 7 days Every 4 days Medium ⭐⭐ Yam 🥔 160g 10 days One-time Medium ⭐

New farm suggestion: Cranberries×80 + Pumpkins×40

docs.title: Fall Festivals
docs.text: Date Festival Key Point Fall 16 Stardew Valley Fair Save Iridium-quality items + Diamonds + Wine in advance Fall 27 Spirit's Eve Get the Golden Pumpkin

docs.title: Fall Key: Prepare for Winter
docs.text:
1. Upgrade sprinklers (prepare for the greenhouse)
  2. Stockpile seeds (for Spring 1 planting rush)
  3. Unlock the greenhouse (if you haven't yet)

docs.title: 🎣 Fall Fishing
docs.text:
Fall fishing adds unique seasonal catches:

Fish Location Time Sell Price (Iridium) Notes Salmon River 6am-7pm 300g Fall-only Walleye River 12pm-2am (rain) 315g Requires rain Tiger Trout River 6am-7pm 300g Also in Gingersnork Angler River (north of farm bridge) N/A 2,000g Legendary fish — one per save Midnight Carp River 10pm-2am 750g Fall/Winter, Sewer

Pro Tip: Use a Trap Bobber + Dish o' the Sea to catch the Angler. Bring the best rod you have and aim for max casting distance.

docs.title: 🏛️ Stardew Valley Fair — 1000 Star Tokens Strategy
docs.text:
The Summer 16 Stardew Valley Fair is your chance to get rare items. Here's how to get 1000 tokens easily:

Best Approach: Fishing mini-game - Takes ~5 minutes at the fishing booth - Easy to score 100+ tokens per attempt with practice - No need to save items all year

Alternative (Item-Based): Bring high-quality items for the grange display: - 9 Items × iridium quality + varied categories = max points - Diamond, Wine, Gold Bar, Iridium-quality crops

Best Purchase: Stardrop (2,000 tokens) — permanent energy boost. Save your tokens for this.

docs.title: 💰 Fall Profit Maximization
docs.text:
Activity Daily Gold Notes Cranberries (200 plants) ~3,000g Continuous harvest, low effort Pumpkin (100) + Keg ~2,500g High profit per plant Ancient Fruit + Keg ~4,000g If unlocked from greenhouse Preserves Jars (Cranberries) +40% value Continuous input Kegs (Hops saved from Summer) ~500g/day Already grown, just process

Bottom Line: Cranberries for steady passive income + Pumpkins (kegged) for big batches = optimal Fall economy. Don't forget to plant corn on Summer 28! It survives into Fall for a head start.

docs.title: 🏠 Farm Layout Planning
docs.text: A good layout = time-saving + profitable + beautiful. This guide provides layout plans for every stage from beginner to endgame.

docs.title: Golden Rules of Layout
docs.text: Rule Description 1. Centralized Irrigation Concentrate farming in one area for maximum sprinkler coverage 2. Optimal Pathing Daily route: House → Mailbox → Farmhouse → Greenhouse → Barn, in a straight line 3. Modular Design Four main zones: Planting Zone + Animal Zone + Processing Zone + Decoration Zone 4. Leave Room Buildings can be moved, but plan roads and tree positions in advance

docs.title: 🌱 Beginner Layout (First Year Spring)
docs.text:
Goal: Maximize planting area with minimal money

│     │  (40 tiles)    │            │
│     │  Parsnip+Potato│            │
│     └───────────────┘            │
│        │                         │
│     ┌──┴────────────┐            │
│     │  Chests+Boxes  │           │
│     │  Furnace+Anvil │           │

│                                  │

└─────────────────────────────────┘

First Spring Focus: Don't worry about layout — earn money and upgrade tools first. Save enough by the end of spring to buy Copper/Iron Sprinklers.

docs.text:
Farm Type Best For Difficulty Standard Farm Beginners ✅ Most space ⭐ Easy Riverland Farm Fishing enthusiasts 🎣 ⭐⭐ Forest Farm Foraging fans 🌲 ⭐⭐ Hill-top Farm Miners ⛏️ ⭐⭐⭐ Wilderness Farm Combat lovers ⚔️ ⭐⭐⭐ Four Corners Farm Multiplayer/Modular ⭐ Beach Farm Challenge players 💪 ⭐⭐⭐⭐

Beginner recommendation: Standard Farm or Four Corners Farm.

docs.title: ❓ Layout FAQ
docs.text:
Q: Do I need to plan the layout all at once? A: No. Buildings can be moved by Robin (free!), but roads and trees can be planned in advance.

Q: When should I start planning the final layout? A: First year winter — by then all the land is cleared and you have some money to spare.

Q: When can I get lots of Iridium Sprinklers? A: After reaching floor 80+ in the mines, open geodes and kill monsters for iridium ore, then craft iridium sprinklers.

docs.title: 🎪 Festival Events Calendar
docs.text:
Stardew Valley has 12 major festivals each year, plus the 3-day Night Market. Every festival offers unique activities, rewards, and seasonal shopping opportunities. This guide lists every festival with complete details.

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6

docs.title: 📅 Quick Reference: Complete Festival Calendar
docs.text: Date Festival Season Location Type Best For Spring 13 Egg Festival 🌸 Spring Town Center Contest + Shop 🥇 Strawberry Seeds Spring 24 Flower Dance 🌸 Spring Cindersap Forest Social + Shop 💞 Romance opportunity Summer 11-13 Night Market ☀️ Summer Beach Shop + Fishing 🐟 Deep-sea fish + Pearl Summer 28 Moonlight Jellies ☀️ Summer Beach Watching 🌙 Decoration reward Fall 16 Stardew Valley Fair 🍁 Fall Town Center Contest + Mini-games 🥇 Stardrop + Star Tokens Fall 27 Spirit's Eve 🍁 Fall Town Square Maze + Shop 🎃 Golden Pumpkin Winter 8 Fishing Tournament ❄️ Winter Beach Contest 🎣 Iridium Rod + Hat Winter 15-17 Night Market ❄️ Winter Beach Shop + Fishing 🐟 Deep-sea fish (repeat!) Winter 25 Feast of the Winter Star ❄️ Winter Lewis' House Social + Gift 🎁 Rare gift items — Desert Festival All Year Desert Shop 🐪 Rare seeds + items

docs.title: Spring 13 — Egg Festival
docs.text:
Detail Info Time 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM Location Town Center (near Community Center) Start Location Talk to Lewis at the Town Center Main Activity Egg Hunt — find 9 hidden eggs in the town Win Condition Find the most eggs (Abigail is the NPC to beat) Win Reward Straw Hat (cosmetic) Participation Reward Straw Hat (win or lose — you still get one)

🛒 Festival Shop (Must-Buy Items)

Item Price Priority Notes Strawberry Seeds 100g each ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Most profitable spring crop — buy 20-50+ Straw Hat 2,000g ⭐⭐ Already earned from participation Decorations 500-1,000g ⭐ Cosmetic only

💰 Strawberry Seed Investment Guide

Playstyle Seeds to Buy Expected Harvests Profit First Year (Spring 13 planted) 20-30 packs 2 harvests (Spring 16 + 20) ~2,000-3,000g First Year (Spring 13, Speed-Gro) 40-50 packs 3 harvests (Spring 16, 20, 24) ~6,000-8,000g Second Year+ 100-200+ packs 4-5 harvests with Deluxe Speed-Gro Massive profit

docs.title: Spring 24 — Flower Dance
docs.text:
Detail Info Time 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM Location Cindersap Forest (south of the Wizard's Tower) Start Location Enter the forest — automatically starts Main Activity Dance with an NPC (requires ≥4 hearts) Reward None (cosmetic only) Secondary Activities Shop for decorations + flowers

💞 How to Dance with an NPC

  1. Give 2 quality gifts per week (Loved gifts preferred)
  2. Talk to them daily
  3. By Spring 24, you need 4 hearts
  4. Easiest NPCs to dance with Year 1:
  5. Haley — Loves Daffodils (foraged) and Sunflowers
  6. Elliott — Loves Duck Feathers or Pomegranate (harder Year 1)
  7. Leah — Loves Salad (buy at Saloon for 220g)
  8. Harvey — Loves Coffee (buy at Saloon for 300g)

Note: Dancing with an NPC gives a massive friendship boost — even if you just stand there awkwardly.

docs.text:
Detail Info Time 10:00 AM – 11:50 PM Location Town Square Main Activity Maze exploration Reward Golden Pumpkin (sell for 2,500g or use as decoration)

🎃 Maze Guide 1. Enter the maze house (looks like a haunted mansion) 2. Navigate through dark corridors 3. Find the exit — the Golden Pumpkin is at the end 4. You can also buy Halloween decorations at the shop

docs.title: Winter 8 — Festival of Ice (Fishing Tournament)
docs.text:
Detail Info Time 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM Location Beach (frozen river) Main Activity Ice fishing competition — catch the biggest fish! Win Condition Catch the heaviest fish (length or weight) 1st Place Prize Choice of Iridium Rod, Fisherman's Hat, Sailor's Cap Strategy Stock up a big fish in advance OR fish early that morning

🎣 How to Win (Guaranteed)

Method Details Method 1 (Cheese) Catch a Legendary Fish earlier in the year, save it in a chest, then enter it at the festival = instant win Method 2 (Day-of) Fish in the Mountain Lake before the festival — catch a Sturgeon or large Largemouth Bass Method 3 (Natural) Compete with the AI — you usually only need a 10+ inch fish to win

docs.title: Money-Making Tips
docs.text:
Priority Fish Reason 🥇 Spring: Catfish 200g each, more river fish in rain 🥈 Summer: Octopus 150g each, but only appears 6AM-1PM 🥉 Year-round: Sturgeon 200g each, pond produces Roe→Aged Roe

Fishing is the most profitable Year 1 skill. Grab the rod on Spring 1, fish every day until 2PM, earning 500-1000g/day easily.

💡 Fish Pond advice: Stock Sturgeon (produces Roe for Caviar) or Squid/Sea Cucumber (for cooking gifts).

docs.title: 🎣 Complete Fishing Guide
docs.text: Fishing is one of the most profitable skills in Stardew Valley — and the fastest way to earn money early on. This guide covers the complete path from fishing novice to legendary fish hunter.

docs.title: Fishing Level Unlocks
docs.text: Level Unlock Description 1 Training Rod 25g, lower quality catch but easier to use 2 Bait Attach bait to rod to reduce bite time 3 Crab Pot Recipe Place crab pots in water to catch creatures 4 Recycling Machine Recycle trash into resources 5 Fisher/Gatherer Fisher: Fish sell for 25% more / Gatherer: Double crab pot yield 6 Spinner + Bait Spinner reduces fish struggle time 7 Treasure Hunter Increases treasure chest spawn rate 8 Cork Bobber Increases fishing bar size 9 Magnet Attracts more treasure chests 10 Mariner/Pirate Mariner: Crab pots catch exclusive fish / Pirate: Double treasure chest chance

docs.title: Fishing Rod Recommendations
docs.text: Rod Price Required Level Description Bamboo Pole Free Starting Can't attach bait Training Rod 25g 1 Good for beginners to practice Fiberglass Rod 1800g 2 Can attach bait Iridium Rod 7500g 6 Can attach bait + tackle Space Rod ? Late game All-purpose rod

docs.title: Days 1-3 (Beginner Phase)
docs.text:
Gear:     Bamboo Pole
Location: Ocean Pier
Strategy: Catch Sardines + Anchovies (easy to catch)
Income:   500-800g/day
Goal:     Reach Fishing Level 2

docs.title: Days 4-7 (Apprentice Phase)
docs.text:
Gear:     Fiberglass Rod + Bait
Location: Lake/River
Strategy: Bait speeds up bite time
Income:   1000-2000g/day
Goal:     Reach Fishing Level 5

docs.title: Days 8-14 (Skilled Phase)
docs.text:
Gear:     Iridium Rod + Bait + Tackle
Location: Rotate River + Ocean + Lake
Strategy: Catch high-value fish for the season
Income:   3000-5000g/day
Goal:     Reach Fishing Level 10

docs.title: Spring
docs.text:
⭐ Most Profitable: Catfish (Rainy days, River)
    Price: 200g+   Difficulty: Medium
    Pair with: Copper pan coffee + Trap Bobber

docs.title: Summer
docs.text:
⭐ Most Profitable: Octopus (6AM-1PM, Ocean)
    Price: 300g+   Difficulty: Extreme
    Recommended: Iridium Rod + Trap Bobber

docs.title: Fall
docs.text:
⭐ Most Profitable: Walleye (Rainy night)
    Price: 300g+   Difficulty: Hard
    Pair with: Spinner + Bait

docs.title: Winter
docs.text:
⭐ Most Profitable: Glacierfish (Seasonal Legendary)
    Price: 1000g+   Difficulty: Extreme
    One chance — make sure you catch it!

docs.title: Fast Leveling Method
docs.text:
1. Carp (Lake) + Sunfish give 50 XP → catch 50 to level up
2. Lucky days (TV fortune) increase treasure chest chance
3. Perfect catches give +20% bonus XP
4. Eat Fish Stew (+1 Fishing Level)
5. Rainy days = more rare fish

docs.title: Best Equipment Combo
docs.text:
Iridium Rod + Bait + Trap Bobber
= 50% faster bite time + 33% slower fish escape speed

docs.title: Legendary Fish Tips
docs.text:
1. Save in advance (after waking up)
  2. Eat Seafoam Pudding (+4 Fishing Level)
  3. Use Trap Bobber (not Lead Bobber)
  4. Stay calm — steady hands matter most

docs.title: 🏝️ Ginger Island Complete Guide
docs.text:
Data version: Stardew Valley 1.6.15 (current) | Source: Stardew Valley Wiki + Carl's Guides | Last verified: 2026-07-04

Ginger Island is the tropical endgame area unlocked after completing the Community Center (any method). It offers the highest-profit crops in the game, exclusive equipment upgrades, challenging puzzles, and the path to becoming the Master Craftsman — the game's ultimate blacksmith profession.

This guide covers every aspect of Ginger Island for all player types: Zero-Spend (complete all bundles), Casual (skip optional challenges), and Speedrun (fastest completion path).

docs.title: 🗺️ How to Get There
docs.text:
Method Requirement Cost Notes Community Center Complete all 6 bundles Free Standard route Joja Warehouse Buy all 4 Community Center packages ~14,750g Faster but costs gold

Once the Community Center is restored (or Joja packages purchased), talk to Kebap at the beach to unlock the boat to Ginger Island. The boat ride is free and available 24/7.

💡 Player Tier Tip: Zero-spend players should prioritize Community Center completion as quickly as possible — it's the only way to access Ginger Island without spending gold on Joja packages.

docs.title: 📍 Island Areas Overview
docs.text:
Ginger Island consists of five distinct areas, each with unique content:

Area Access Key Features The Beach (entry) Boat dock Oasis, Island Trader, Master Craftsmen quest giver The Jungle North of Beach Vine-covered paths, Secret Woods puzzle, parrots The Volcano Far East Lava lakes, Qi's gems, floor challenges, final reward The Field Office Jungle Wilbur's research, crop growth tracker The Daily Burrito Beach Restaurant run by Linus, free meals

docs.title: 🏗️ Area 1: The Beach — Your Island Base
The Island Trader
docs.text:
The Island Trader is the most unique shop in the game. She accepts no gold — only special currency items:

Item Offered Currency Required Notes Slime Eggs 1 Slime Egg + 1 Prismatic Shard For slime incubators Coconut 1 Coconut Start your plantation Ancient Fruit Sapling 100 Coconut ~50,000g investment Sugar Candy 1 Sugar Candy Gift for any NPC Sturgeon Egg 1 Sturgeon Egg For fish tanks Island Exclusive Items Various See full table below

Island Trader Exchange Table:

You Give You Receive 1 Crab Pot 1 Crab Pot (trade any crab pot for any other) 1 Parrot Fish 1 Ancient Fruit Sapling 1 Squid Ink 1 Coconut 1 Glazed Yams 1 Sugar Candy 1 Crystal Fruit 1 Slime Egg 1 Aquamarine 1 Ancient Fruit Sapling 1 Omni Geode 1 Random Island Item 1 Frozen Tear 1 Random Island Item 1 Diamond 1 Random Island Item 1 Prismatic Shard 1 Random Island Item

💡 Strategy: Save your Prismatic Shards for Ancient Fruit Saplings. Each shard + 100 coconuts = one sapling. This is the single best investment on the island.

docs.title: The Oasis
docs.text:
Located east of the beach entrance, the Oasis contains:

  • Ancient Seed Production — Buy Ancient Seeds from the man in the hut (requires completing the Field Office crop tracker first)
  • Island Degen-Tree — A mysterious tree that drops random items
  • Waterfall Cave entrance — Leads to the Volcano

docs.title: The Daily Burrito
docs.text:
Linus runs a restaurant here. He prepares free burritos that restore energy and health:

Burrito Type Effect Availability Daily Burrito +400 energy, +100 health Every day Special Burrito Temporary buff (varies) Random

💡 Zero-Spend players: Eat the Daily Burrito every morning to save on energy drinks and food purchases.

docs.title: The Vine-Covered Wall Puzzle
docs.text:
North of the beach, a massive wall of vines blocks the path to the Volcano. You must cut through it using the Kyris' War Axe (upgraded axe from the Volcano).

Path progression:

  1. Cut initial vine wall → reveals the Secret Woods
  2. Solve the Secret Woods Puzzle → unlocks the Hidden Shrine
  3. Continue cutting vines → reaches the Volcano entrance

docs.title: 🔐 Secret Woods Puzzle
docs.text:
Inside the Secret Woods, you'll find three stone statues that must be activated in the correct order. The solution is hidden in the environment:

Solution: Look for the glowing symbols on the ground near the statues. The correct order is:

  1. Sun symbol (top statue)
  2. Moon symbol (left statue)
  3. Star symbol (right statue)

After solving, a door opens revealing the Hidden Shrine.

⚠️ Wrong order? The puzzle resets. No penalty, just try again.

docs.title: 🙏 Hidden Shrine
docs.text:
Once the Secret Woods puzzle is solved, the Hidden Shrine becomes accessible. Here you can offer Prismatic Shards to receive powerful rewards:

Offering Reward Notes 1 Prismatic Shard Slime Hutch Key Build a slime pond on the island 3 Prismatic Shards Qi's Gem Required for Volcano floors 5 Prismatic Shards Island Special Crate Contains rare seeds 10 Prismatic Shards Master Craftsman Recipe Ultimate blacksmith upgrade

💡 Speedrun tip: Collect Prismatic Shards from Qi's Gems, Volcano floor chests, and the Island Degen-Tree. Prioritize the 10-shard offering for the Master Craftsman path.

docs.title: Volcano Floor Structure
docs.text:
The Volcano has four floors, each progressively harder:

Floor Key Feature Reward Difficulty Floor 1 Lava bridges, fire geodes Qi Gem (1st) Easy Floor 2 Fire gauntlet, magma creatures Qi Gem (2nd) Medium Floor 3 Diamond gauntlet, elite enemies Qi Gem (3rd) Hard Floor 4 Final challenge, boss fight Master Craftsman Expert

docs.title: Floor 1 — Warm Up
docs.text:
• Navigate lava bridges using stepping stones
  • Collect Fire Geodes from the ground
  • Defeat the first Magma Spouter enemy
  • Reward: 1 Qi Gem + access to Floor 2

docs.title: Floor 2 — Fire Gauntlet
docs.text:
• Dodge fire projectiles from enemies
  • Collect Ignient drops for crafting
  • Navigate moving platforms over lava
  • Reward: 1 Qi Gem + access to Floor 3

docs.title: Floor 3 — Diamond Gauntlet
docs.text:
• Elite enemies drop Diamonds
  • Complex platforming over lava pools
  • Collect Prismatic Shards from chest rewards
  • Reward: 1 Qi Gem + access to Floor 4

docs.title: Floor 4 — The Final Test
docs.text:
• Face the Volcano Guardian (boss)
  • The Guardian has two phases:
  • Phase 1: Physical attacks (use sword)
  • Phase 2: Fire breath (use shield/block)
  • After defeating, receive the Master Craftsman title

docs.title: 💎 Qi's Gems
docs.text:
You collect 4 Qi Gems across the Volcano floors. These are used to:

  1. Unlock the Island Special Crate at the Field Office
  2. Trade with the Island Trader for rare items
  3. Activate the Daily Burrito special menu

💡 Casual players: You don't need all 4 Qi Gems to access the most profitable island content. Focus on Ancient Fruit and Hops first.

docs.title: 🏆 Master Craftsman Quest
docs.text:
The Master Craftsman is the ultimate blacksmith profession in Stardew Valley. Unlock it by:

  1. Completing all four Volcano floors
  2. Defeating the Volcano Guardian
  3. Returning to the Island Trader with 10 Prismatic Shards
  4. Purchasing the Master Craftsman Recipe

docs.title: What Does Master Craftsman Do?
docs.text: Benefit Description Gold-infused Tools Your tools gain a golden glow and +10% efficiency Master Smithing All artisan goods sell for +25% more Exclusive Recipes Access to the Galaxy Weapon upgrade path Island Title Your character displays a golden crown on the island

docs.title: Best Use of Master Craftsman Profession
docs.text:
Player Type Strategy Zero-Spend Focus on Ancient Fruit Wine (highest profit per keg) Casual Combine with Wine Maker profession for +40% total bonus Speedrun Take Gold profession early, switch to Artisan later for stacking

⚠️ Important: Master Craftsman profession stacks multiplicatively with other bonuses. Combined with Wine Maker profession, Ancient Fruit Wine sells for approximately 2,310g per keg instead of the base 1,650g.

docs.title: Ancient Fruit — The King of Ginger Island
docs.text:
Stat Value Growth Time 28 days (initial), then 5 days regrowth Base Sell Price 550g (fruit) Wine Price (Keg) 1,650g Gold Wine Price 2,062g Daily Profit (regrowth) 110g/day raw, 330g/day wine Lifetime Yield (1 season) ~10 fruits per plant

Optimal Layout:

Layout Quantity Investment Daily Wine Profit Small 20 plants ~20,000g (seeds + irrigation) ~6,600g/day Medium 50 plants ~50,000g ~16,500g/day Large 100+ plants ~100,000g ~33,000g/day

💡 Zero-Spend: Start with 20 Ancient Fruit plants. Harvest wine for 3-4 seasons before expanding. 💡 Speedrun: Plant 100+ immediately after unlocking island. Use Gold Sprinklers for maximum coverage.

docs.title: Hops — The Fast Cash Machine
docs.text:
Stat Value Growth Time 11 days initial, then daily regrowth Base Sell Price 25g per hop Pale Ale Price (Keg) 300g Daily Profit (raw) 25g/day per tile Daily Profit (Pale Ale) ~200g/day per tile

Optimal Layout:

Layout Quantity Daily Pale Ale Profit Small 30 hops ~6,000g/day Medium 80 hops ~16,000g/day Large 200+ hops ~40,000g/day

⚠️ Note: Hops only grow in Summer and Fall. On Ginger Island, they grow year-round since the climate is tropical.

docs.title: Starfruit — Late-Game Luxury
docs.text:
Stat Value Growth Time 13 days, no regrowth Base Sell Price 750g Wine Price (Keg) 3,150g Daily Profit (wine) 450g/day

Starfruit is the highest-profit single-harvest crop but doesn't regrow. Best for players with many kegs but limited land.

docs.title: Island Special Crates
docs.text:
Found throughout the jungle, these crates contain rare seeds:

Crate Contents Probability Ancient Fruit 2% Starfruit 3% Coffee Bean 5% Pineapple 4% Taro Root 3% Papaya 2%

💡 Tip: Place crates near your house for easy daily collection. Use a Scarecrow to protect nearby crops.

docs.title: Pineapple & Papaya
docs.text:
These two crops are exclusive to Ginger Island:

Crop Growth Time Sell Price Regrowth Pineapple 🍍 14 days 150g Every 7 days Papaya 🥭 12 days 300g Every 4 days

Papaya is the best non-Ancient Fruit crop on the island. At 300g base with 4-day regrowth, it produces 75g/day raw and 225g/day as juice (Preserves Jar).

docs.title: Parrots
docs.text:
Parrots roam the jungle and will steal items from your backpack. They can be tamed with:

Food Effect Wild Seeds 50% tame chance Quartz 80% tame chance Diamond 100% tame chance

Tamed parrots follow you and occasionally drop Prismatic Shards.

docs.title: 📊 Profit Comparison — Ginger Island Crops
docs.text:
All prices assume no profession bonuses. Wine/Juice prices assume basic Keg/Preserves Jar.

Crop Growth Regrowth Raw/g Day Wine/juice/g Day Best For Ancient Fruit 28+5d Every 5d 110g 330g Endgame Starfruit 13d No 57.7g 242g High-value single Papaya 12+4d Every 4d 75g 225g (juice) Mid-game Hops 11d Daily 25g 200g (ale) Fast cash Pineapple 14+7d Every 7d 21.4g 64.3g (wine) Steady income Blueberry 13+4d Every 4d 20.8g 62.4g (wine) Early island

🏆 Verdict: Ancient Fruit Wine is the single most profitable long-term investment. Papaya Juice is the best mid-game option.

docs.title: Zero-Spend Path (Free-to-Complete)
docs.text:
Day 1-7:   Complete Community Center → unlock Ginger Island
Day 8-14:  Plant 20 Ancient Fruit + 30 Hops
Day 15-30: Harvest wine, save all gold
Day 31+:   Expand to 50 Ancient Fruit, add Papaya

Budget allocation: - 10,000g → Ancient Fruit Saplings (from Island Trader) - 5,000g → Kegs (build with wood) - 0g → Everything else is free through farming

Expected profit after Year 1: ~500,000g (with Ancient Fruit wine)

docs.title: Casual Path (Balanced Investment)
docs.text:
Week 1:    Buy Joja package → unlock island faster
Week 2:    Plant 50 Ancient Fruit + 80 Hops
Week 3:    Build 20 Kegs, start wine production
Week 4+:   Expand to 100 Ancient Fruit, add Starfruit

Budget allocation: - 14,750g → Joja packages (skip CC grind) - 50,000g → Ancient Fruit + Hops + Papaya - 30,000g → Kegs, Sprinklers, Irrigation - 20,000g → Starfruit seeds (late game)

Expected monthly profit: ~100,000g/day with full production

docs.title: Speedrun Path (Maximum Efficiency)
docs.text:
Days 1-3:  Skip all dialogue, focus on Community Center bundles
Days 4-7:  Island unlocked, plant EVERYTHING
Days 8-14: Clear Volcano floors, get Master Craftsman
Days 15+:  Full Ancient Fruit + Hops + Starfruit rotation

Priority order: 1. Community Center (fastest bundle completion) 2. Volcano Floors 1-4 (Master Craftsman ASAP) 3. Ancient Fruit plantation (max tiles) 4. Hops + Papaya (supplemental income) 5. Starfruit (endgame luxury)

Time to Master Craftsman: ~14 in-game days Time to 100,000g/day: ~30 in-game days

docs.title: 🏅 Achievements Related to Ginger Island
docs.text: Achievement Requirement Difficulty A New Ally Complete Master Craftsman quest Medium Full House Fill all six slots in the Island Trader's crane Hard Treasure Hunter Find 100 treasures via scuba diving Easy Legendary Fish Catch all 5 Legendary Fish Expert

docs.text:

  • [ ] Talk to Island Trader

docs.title: Stardew Valley Greenhouse Crops Guide
docs.text: The greenhouse is one of the most valuable assets in Stardew Valley. Once repaired (via the Pantry Bundles or purchasing from JojaMart), it provides a 24-tile by 12-tile indoor farming space where crops grow regardless of season. This guide covers the best crops for year-round profit, detailed profit analysis, layout optimization, and expert tips to turn your greenhouse into a goldmine.

docs.title: Why the Greenhouse Matters
docs.text: Unlike outdoor plots, the greenhouse offers: - Season-independent growth – Plant any crop any time. - No weather risks – Storms, crows, or winter won't affect your harvest. - Permanent trellis placement – Trellis crops (like hops or green beans) can be placed on the edges without blocking movement. - Sprinkler efficiency – The rectangular shape allows for optimal sprinkler layouts.

docs.title: Best Crops for Year-Round Profit
docs.text: Profitability in the greenhouse depends on gold per day (GPD) and processing potential (e.g., kegs, preserves jars). Below are the top contenders.

docs.title: Top 5 Crops by Raw Profit
docs.text: Crop Growth Time Regrowth Base Sell Price Gold/Day (No Processing) Notes Ancient Fruit 28 days initial, then 7 days Yes 550g 78.6g Best overall; kegs yield 1,650g per wine Starfruit 13 days No 750g 57.7g Higher base price but no regrowth; best with kegs Blueberry 13 days initial, then 4 days Yes 50g per berry 20.8g High volume; good for preserves jars Cranberry 7 days initial, then 5 days Yes 75g per berry 18.9g Reliable early-game option Pineapple 14 days initial, then 7 days Yes 300g 42.9g Solid mid-game choice (Ginger Island)

docs.title: Processed Profit Analysis
docs.text:
Processing crops through kegs or preserves jars multiplies profits dramatically. Here's the GPD when using kegs (assuming Artisan profession):

Crop Base Wine Price Keg Time (Days) GPD (Processed) Ancient Fruit 1,650g 7 235.7g Starfruit 2,250g 7 321.4g Pineapple 900g 7 128.6g Blueberry (3 berries) 450g (3 wines) 7 64.3g Cranberry (2 berries) 300g (2 wines) 7 42.9g

Verdict: Starfruit has the highest GPD when processed, but Ancient Fruit requires less replanting and is more sustainable. For maximum profit, fill your greenhouse with Starfruit and process into wine. For low-maintenance, go with Ancient Fruit.

docs.title: Optimal Sprinkler Placement
docs.text:
The greenhouse interior is 12 tiles wide by 10 tiles tall (usable soil). The ideal layout uses Quality Sprinklers (4-tile radius) or Iridium Sprinklers (8-tile radius).

Iridium Sprinkler Layout (Best): - Place 4 Iridium Sprinklers in a 2x2 grid. - Each covers a 5x5 area (24 crops per sprinkler). - Total crops: 96 (with 4 sprinklers). - Leave a 1-tile border for walking.

Quality Sprinkler Layout (Budget): - Place 8 Quality Sprinklers in a 3x3 pattern (center empty). - Each covers 8 crops. - Total crops: 64. - Requires more manual watering early on.

docs.title: Trellis Crops Placement
docs.text: Trellis crops (e.g., hops, green beans, grapes) block movement. Place them along the edges of the greenhouse (the 1-tile border) so you can still walk through the center. For example: - Plant hops on the top row (y=1) and bottom row (y=10). - Use the center for non-trellis crops.

docs.title: Keg and Preserves Jar Integration
docs.text:
Place kegs or preserves jars inside the greenhouse to save space in your farmhouse or shed. Use the 1-tile border around the soil for kegs. A typical layout: - 4 Iridium Sprinklers in the center. - 24 kegs along the perimeter (6 per side). - 96 crops in the soil.

This setup allows you to harvest and process without leaving the greenhouse.

docs.title: Year-Round Ancient Fruit Farm
docs.text:
Once you obtain an Ancient Seed (from artifact spots, fishing, or the Traveling Cart), plant it in the greenhouse. After the first harvest (28 days), it regrows every 7 days. With 96 plants, you'll harvest 96 fruits per week. Process into wine for 1,650g each = 158,400g per week.

Tip: Use a Seed Maker to turn some Ancient Fruit into seeds. Plant them in the greenhouse until full, then sell the rest.

docs.title: Starfruit Mega-Profits
docs.text:
Starfruit seeds cost 400g each (from Sandy in the Calico Desert). With 96 plants, that's 38,400g upfront. Harvest every 13 days. Process into wine (2,250g each) = 216,000g per harvest. Replant immediately.

Downside: Requires constant replanting and seed purchases. Best for late-game when money is abundant.

docs.title: Mixed Crop Strategy
docs.text:
If you're early-game, mix crops for variety: - Plant 48 Blueberry bushes for steady income. - Plant 48 Cranberries for fall-style profits. - Use preserves jars for Blueberries (2 days) and kegs for Cranberries (7 days).

This diversifies your income and reduces risk.

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
• Always use the Artisan profession – It increases processed goods value by 40%.
  • Place a chest inside the greenhouse – Store seeds, fertilizer, and tools for quick access.
  • Use Deluxe Speed-Gro – Reduces growth time by 25% (e.g., Starfruit from 13 to 10 days). Buy from Pierre or craft.
  • Don't plant trees inside the greenhouse – They take up valuable crop space. Use the farmhouse or the railroad area for trees.
  • Harvest with a Gold or Iridium Scythe – Speeds up harvesting for multi-harvest crops like Blueberries.
  • Automate with Junimo Huts – Not possible in the greenhouse, but you can use the Automate mod (PC) to connect chests to kegs.
  • Save one of each crop – For quests, bundles, or the Stardew Valley Fair grange display.
  • Check the Traveling Cart – Buy Rare Seeds (Sweet Gem Berry) for a one-time profit of 3,000g each (but not regrowable).

With this guide, your greenhouse will become the engine of your farm's economy. Happy farming!

docs.title: 🎩 Complete Hat Collection Guide
docs.text:
Hats are purely cosmetic headwear in Stardew Valley — they provide no stat benefits but are essential for fashion-conscious farmers and completionists. There are 86+ hats available in Stardew Valley 1.6, acquired through events, shops, fishing treasure, monsters, and secret quests.

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6
